Create a comparison table that highlights your product features as the direct antidote to those frustrations. Refine the terminology until your brand’s advantages look like essential requirements rather than optional features.
what_it_does: Drafts 'Us vs. Them' comparison tables that highlight non-obvious proprietary advantages.

name: The Cross-Sell Weaver
command: Identify three complementary items for this product and draft a 'Perfect Pairing' description that explains the functional synergy. Continuously adjust the tone to be helpful rather than salesy, checking if the bundle solves a problem the single product doesn't. Stop when the bundle offer feels like a logical upgrade to the user's workflow.
what_it_does: Dynamically generates 'Frequently Bought Together' copy that feels essential rather than random.

name: The FAQ Value Extractor
command: Review recent support tickets or FAQ queries and translate the most common question into a proactive sentence for the main description. Refine this sentence so it adds value to the sales pitch rather than just answering a question. Stop when the top three friction points are addressed before the user reaches the FAQ section.
what_it_does: Converts repetitive customer support questions into proactive sales copy on the PDP.

name: The Ghost-Benefit Finder
command: Brainstorm five ways customers might use this product that aren't listed on the box. For each use, draft a one-sentence sales pitch and evaluate its market size. Stop when you find one 'secret' benefit that could serve as the basis for a niche sub-collection or landing page.
what_it_does: Discovers non-intended uses for products that could be secret marketing goldmines.

name: The Lifestyle-Context Injector
command: Write three brief scenarios where this product solves a specific moment of everyday frustration. Continue refining these vignettes to be more sensory and emotional, ensuring they speak to the target persona's aspirations. Stop once the product description feels like a story about the user’s future rather than a spec sheet.
what_it_does: Adds 'in-use' scenarios to product copy to help customers visualize ownership.

name: The Micro-Copy Humanizer
command: Take a standard functional string like 'Add to Cart' or 'Check Out' and rewrite it in the style of the brand's persona. Assess if the new version adds personality without sacrificing clarity, and iterate until the micro-copy matches the brand's 'vibe' perfectly. Stop once all transactional buttons feel conversational.
what_it_does: Transforms generic checkout and button text into brand-consistent, high-conversion language.

name: The Packaging Experience Polish
command: Outline the step-by-step physical experience of opening the product, identifying one 'moment of delight' that could be added (like a note or sticker). Refine the copy on the insert card to be highly shareable and include a clear social CTA. Stop when the unboxing sequence feels worth filming for an Instagram story.
what_it_does: Designs the post-purchase unboxing experience to encourage social sharing and reviews.

name: The Returns Reducer
command: Examine the shared reasons for product returns and rewrite the 'Specs' section to include 'Best for' and 'Not for' scenarios. After each draft, pretend you are a customer trying to find a reason to be disappointed and fix that gap. Stop when the description provides zero ambiguity regarding size, feel, or compatibility.
what_it_does: Injects sizing and material nuance into descriptions to lower return rates for apparel or furniture.

name: The Seasonal Pivot Loop
command: Identify the upcoming holiday and integrate its core themes into the product's primary hero section. Review the copy to ensure it creates urgency for the event while keeping the core evergreen keywords intact. Repeat the balancing act until the listing feels timely for the holiday but still ranks for its main search terms.
what_it_does: Rapidly updates product listings for specific holidays or events without losing original SEO rankings.

name: The SEO Intent Matcher
command: Look at the top 3 ranking URLs for your target keyword and identify their primary 'intent hook.' Rewrite your meta-description to include those hooks while maintaining a unique value proposition, then grade your own work against the competitor's clarity. Repeat this refinement until your CTR-bait score objectively exceeds the current market leaders.
what_it_does: Iteratively aligns meta-titles with transactional search intent by checking against top-ranking competitors.

name: The Subscription Stickiness Loop
command: Draft a 'Why Subscribe' section that focuses on convenience and exclusive perks rather than just price. Continue to refine the language to emphasize the long-term relationship and ease of management. Stop when the subscription offer feels like a 'club' membership rather than just a recurring bill.
what_it_does: Refines the 'Subscribe & Save' value proposition to prevent immediate cancellations.

name: The Trust-Batch Validator
command: Research the top anxieties of this product’s specific sub-niche (e.g., keto, vegan, heavy-duty). Generate a list of five specific trust badges or certifications that directly address those anxieties, and refine their placement for the product page. Stop when the trust signals cover every major safety or ethical concern a lead might have.
what_it_does: Identifies the specific certifications or icons that will actually convert your target demographic.

name: The UGC Prompt Generator
command: Analyze the product's most complex feature and draft a 15-second script prompt for a customer to follow in a video review. Refine the prompt to ensure the customer mentions the specific benefit without sounding scripted. Stop when you have a set of three 'Review Missions' that cover the entire product experience.
what_it_does: Creates hyper-specific instructions for customers to record video reviews that hit key selling points.

name: The Viral Hooks Smith
command: Generate five variations of a 3-second opening hook for a product video based on current viral trends. Evaluate each for immediate 'pattern interrupt' potential and rewrite the weakest ones until all five possess a high probability of stopping a scroll. Stop when you have three hooks ready for split-testing.
what_it_does: Generates and filters product opening lines based on high-retention TikTok and Reel trends.

name: The 5-Minute Lab Spinner
command: Identify a complex science lab that requires expensive equipment and 60 minutes. Suggest an alternative using only 'kitchen' materials and taking only 10 minutes. Iterate on the design to ensure the core 'Aha!' moment of the original experiment is preserved. Stop when the experiment is safe, cheap, and demonstrates the scientific law perfectly.
what_it_does: Transforms high-resource science experiments into fast, low-cost activities.

name: The Analogical Leapfrog
command: Propose an analogy for this concept using a topic students love (e.g., gaming, TikTok, sports). Evaluate the analogy's accuracy: if it is less than 90% conceptually accurate, pivot to a new pop-culture domain and try again. Continue until you have three distinct, high-fidelity analogies from three completely different sub-cultures.
what_it_does: Connects abstract curriculum points to 10 wildly different pop-culture domains.

name: The Anti-Cheat Paraphraser
command: Take this standard essay prompt and add a 'Contextual Constraint' (e.g., must reference a specific local landmark or class discussion). Evaluate if an AI could still generate a generic answer. Keep adding specific, hyper-local, or personal-reflection requirements until a generic AI response would fail the rubric's 'Specific Evidence' criteria.
what_it_does: Modifies essay prompts so they require specific, lived-experience data that LLMs can't guess.

name: The Bloom’s Elevator
command: Start with a 'Remembering' level question for this topic. In each subsequent turn, rewrite the question to hit the next level of Bloom's Taxonomy (Understand, Apply, Analyze, Evaluate, Create). Ensure the difficulty increases at each step and stop only when you have a 'Create' level prompt that requires synthesizing 3 or more concepts.
what_it_does: Takes a simple multiple-choice question and iterates it up the hierarchy of thinking.

name: The Cognitive Friction Balancer
command: Analyze this lesson segment for cognitive load, then rewrite it to be 10% simpler while maintaining the core objective. Continue this refinement process, alternating between simplifying the language and deepening the conceptual challenge, until the explanation achieves a readability grade of 8 without losing academic rigor. After each iteration, output the current 'Friction Score' and the revised text.
what_it_does: Adjusts lesson complexity iteratively to keep students in the 'Zone of Proximal Development.'

name: The Cross-Curricular Weaver
command: Take this History lesson and find a way to integrate a specific 8th-grade Math standard (e.g., data visualization). Modify the activity to naturally require both skills, then iterate by adding an ELA literacy component. Stop when the lesson hits three distinct subject standards while remaining under the 45-minute time limit.
what_it_does: Embeds secondary-subject skills into your primary lesson without losing focus.

name: The Debate-Opponent Bot
command: Review this student's debate position. Generate the 'Weakest' counter-argument first for them to practice on, then gradually increase the logical complexity of the counters. After each round, ask the student to respond, and continue the 'logical escalation' until they can successfully defend their point against a sophisticated, multi-factor critique.
what_it_does: Generates increasingly difficult counter-arguments to prepare students for a debate.

name: The Error-Pattern Detective
command: Examine this list of 5 student mistakes and hypothesize the single underlying conceptual misunderstanding common to all of them. Generate a remedial task designed to fix that specific root cause, then predict how a student would fail that task if the misconception is not yet cleared. Repeat the analysis until the remedial task is bulletproof against the identified error pattern.
what_it_does: Analyzes student mistakes to find the 'root-cause' misconception.

name: The Evidence-Climb Loop
command: Take a basic student claim (e.g., 'climate change is bad') and suggest a piece of evidence. Evaluate it: if the evidence is anecdotal, suggest a 'Data-Driven' piece; if it's general data, suggest a 'Peer-Reviewed' source. Continue this 'Evidence Climb' until the claim is supported by three different levels of authoritative sources.
what_it_does: Trains students to strengthen their arguments by forcing them to find higher-quality evidence.

name: The Exit-Ticket Refiner
command: Draft a 1-minute exit ticket question for this lesson. Analyze if a student could answer it correctly using only 'memorized' info without 'conceptual' understanding. Keep refining the question—adding a 'Why?' or 'What if?' requirement—until the question effectively separates 'mimicry' from 'mastery.'
what_it_does: Crafts a single question that proves beyond doubt whether a student 'got it.'

name: The Feedback Distiller
command: Read this paragraph of teacher feedback and extract the one most impactful 'Next Step.' Re-read the student's original work and refine that 'Next Step' so it is achievable in exactly 5 minutes. Continue refining until the feedback is a single, clear command that even a struggling student could follow immediately without further clarification.
what_it_does: Turns verbose teacher comments into actionable, single-task increments for students.

name: The Gamified Quest-Log
command: Map these five curriculum benchmarks to a fantasy or sci-fi quest archetype (e.g., 'The Gathering,' 'The Siege'). For each benchmark, write a 'flavor-text' introduction that gives the student a role and a goal. Iterate until the tasks feel like game objectives rather than school work, while ensuring the academic output remains unchanged.
what_it_does: Turns a standard reading list into a narrative-driven RPG questline.

name: The Graphic-Organizer Architect
command: Analyze this text and determine if it follows a 'Cause/Effect,' 'Compare/Contrast,' 'Sequence,' or 'Cycle' logic. Propose a specific layout for a graphic organizer. Iterate the layout instructions until they are so precise that a student could draw the organizer on a blank piece of paper based on your description alone.
what_it_does: Suggests the perfect visual structure for any complex piece of information.

name: The Inquiry-Spark Generator
command: Look at the standard objective 'Students will learn X.' Rewrite it as a 'Curiosity Gap' question—something a student would actually want to solve (e.g., 'Why do most empires fail in the same year?'). Repeat until the question sounds more like a Netflix documentary title than a school curriculum point, without losing the link to the standard.
what_it_does: Turns a standard curriculum objective into a 'Mystery and Mission' task.

name: The Micro-Lecture Trimmer
command: Review this lecture transcript and identify the 'Big Idea' and 'Small Win.' Remove all filler words, repeated examples, and tangents. Keep trimming the word count until the entire script can be read at a moderate pace in exactly 180 seconds, without losing the essential 'how-to' steps.
what_it_does: Condenses 20-minute lectures into high-impact 3-minute mini-lessons.

name: The Misconception-Preemptor
command: Look at this new Math/Science concept and list three ways a student might logically (but incorrectly) apply their current knowledge to it. For each, draft a 'Pre-emptive Strike'—a specific sentence or example to use during the direct instruction that addresses the error before it happens. Iterate until the instruction feels 'immune' to these specific errors.
what_it_does: Predicts the exact way a student will misunderstand a new concept.

name: The Neurodivergent UX Audit
command: Scan this assignment for 'instructional noise' and remove one non-essential formatting element or sentence in each pass. Continue until only the critical action steps and essential context remain, ensuring the final version has at least 40% white space. Present the 'clutter-free' version after each reduction along with a justification for what was cut.
what_it_does: Iteratively strips visual and linguistic clutter from worksheets to support executive function.

name: The Parent-Translator Loop
command: Take this professional observation about a student's behavioral or academic deficit and rewrite it for a parent email. Ensure it is empathetic, avoids all education acronyms, and provides one clear way they can help at home. Iterate until the tone is exactly 50% professional and 50% supportive, with no 'blame' language included.
what_it_does: Converts 'Edu-speak' into supportive, jargon-free emails for parents.

name: The Resource-Maximizer
command: Identify the core concepts in this $200 textbook chapter. Search for and provide links to Open Educational Resources (OER), YouTube simulations, and free museum archives that cover the same material. Iterate the list, replacing one 'passive' resource with one 'interactive' resource in each round, until the list is entirely comprised of free, high-engagement materials.
what_it_does: Finds free, high-quality digital alternatives to expensive school textbooks.

name: The Retrieval-Practice Bot
command: Create 5 questions about today's lesson, but ensure 2 of them actually link back to a lesson from three weeks ago. Iterate on the questions to make them 'schema-connectors'—questions that require students to relate the new info to the old info. Stop once you have a 5-question 'Power-Up' that fits on a single sticky note.
what_it_does: Generates 'Low-Stakes' quizzes that prioritize long-term memory over short-term cramming.

name: The Rubric Stress-Tester
command: Create a theoretical student response that follows the letter of your rubric but misses the spirit of the assignment entirely. Refine the rubric after each 'bad' response to close the loophole, then generate a new, even more creative way to 'cheat' the current criteria. Stop once you can no longer find a way to get a high grade without demonstrating actual mastery.
what_it_does: Simulates 10 'bad-faith' student submissions to find loopholes in your grading criteria.

name: The Rubric-to-Plain-English Loop
command: Take a 5-point formal rubric and rewrite the 'Excellent' column into a personal goal-setting statement (e.g., 'I can...'). Then, rewrite the 'Developing' column into a 'Getting there' statement. Iterate until the language is entirely positive and growth-minded, focusing on what is present rather than what is missing.
what_it_does: Explains grading criteria in language a 10-year-old would find encouraging.

name: The Scraped-Knee Narrative
command: Draft a 60-second opening 'hook' for this lesson that uses a real-world high-stakes mystery. Iterate on the hook by increasing the sensory details and lowering the abstract language until the narrative feels urgent and a student would find it 'unskippable.' Stop when the intro successfully connects a dry curriculum standard to a primal human survival or social instinct.
what_it_does: Refines lesson introductions to be visceral, high-stakes, and impossible to ignore.

name: The SEL Moment-Finder
command: Scan this literature lesson or historical event for a 'Decision Point' where a character/figure felt a strong emotion or social pressure. Draft a 2-minute 'Pause for Reflection' question that connects that moment to a student's own life. Iterate until the question is open-ended and avoids being 'preachy' while still targeting a specific SEL competency (e.g., empathy).
what_it_does: Identifies opportunities for Social-Emotional Learning within academic content.

name: The Socratic Trap Builder
command: Identify a common misconception for this topic and draft a question that triggers it; then, draft a follow-up question that reveals the internal contradiction. Keep generating deeper layers of follow-up questions until you have a five-step sequence that forces a student to self-correct their initial premise. Review the sequence after each step to ensure the logic remains inescapable.
what_it_does: Develops a sequence of questioning that leads students to discover their own logical fallacies.

name: The Substitute-Teacher Safeguard
command: Write lesson instructions as if the substitute teacher has zero knowledge of the subject. Identify any point where a sub might get asked a question they can't answer and include a 'Cheat Sheet' response for that point. Iterate until the instructions are so clear that a 12-year-old could lead the class using only this document if the teacher was absent.
what_it_does: Refines lesson instructions until they are impossible for a non-expert to mess up.

name: The Tiered-Support Scaler
command: Take this core task and generate three variants: 'Scaffolded' (with sentence starters), 'Standard,' and 'Extension' (with a creative twist). After each set, check that all three still address the same primary learning objective. Iterate until the extension version requires no more than 10 minutes more than the standard version but significantly more 'Evaluation' level thinking.
what_it_does: Creates three versions of the same task for high, medium, and low-support groups.

name: The Vocab-Immersion Script
command: Write a 200-word story that uses these 5 vocabulary words. After the first draft, analyze if the context clues are strong enough for a reader to guess the meaning of each word. Rewrite the story, strengthening the context clues each time, until a 10-year-old could define all 5 words without a dictionary based solely on the text.
what_it_does: Embeds 5 tier-three vocabulary words into a narrative that explains their meaning via context.

name: The API Ergonomics Polisher
command: Put yourself in the shoes of a junior dev using this API for the first time. Identify one confusing parameter name, inconsistent return type, or redundant argument. Fix it and re-evaluate the overall intuitive flow of the interface until the API feels 'self-documenting'.
what_it_does: Refines function signatures and naming for maximum developer experience.

name: The Boilerplate Burner
command: Scan the codebase for structural patterns repeated three or more times. In each iteration, implement a generic function, mixin, or higher-order component to consolidate one set of duplicates. Continue until no three blocks of code share more than 70% structural similarity.
what_it_does: Identifies and eliminates repetitive code patterns through abstraction.

name: The Bundle Size Shrinker
command: Scan the import list for large external libraries. For each, either identify a way to tree-shake it, replace it with a lightweight alternative, or implement the needed logic natively. Repeat until the theoretical bundle size is minimized for current functionality.
what_it_does: Reduces frontend bundle sizes by identifying unused code and heavy dependencies.

name: The Complexity Crusher
command: Calculate the cyclomatic complexity of the target function. In each iteration, decompose the most complex conditional or loop into a named helper function or a guard clause. Repeat until the complexity score of every individual function is below 5.
what_it_does: Reduces the cyclomatic complexity of functions through recursive simplification.

name: The Concurrency Calibrator
command: Locate a shared state that is accessed by multiple asynchronous or parallel processes. Identify one potential race condition or deadlock risk, and apply a locking mechanism or an immutable pattern. Repeat until all shared state access is synchronized or eliminated.
what_it_does: Identifies race conditions and ensures thread safety in parallel code.

name: The CSS Specificity Sniper
command: Identify the selector with the highest specificity or an '!important' tag. Refactor the styles to use flatter, BEM-like classes or CSS variables instead. Continue until no '!important' tags remain and max nesting depth is 3.
what_it_does: Refactors CSS to eliminate !important tags and deep nesting.

name: The Dependency Auditor
command: List all top-level dependencies. For each, check its last update date, open issue count, and known vulnerabilities. Suggest a 'Keep', 'Update', or 'Replace' action for one library per iteration until the entire dependency tree is healthy and modern.
what_it_does: Evaluates third-party libraries for health, size, and security.

name: The Dependency Decoupler
command: Analyze the import graph and identify the most coupled module. In each step, extract one interface or shared abstraction to break a single link, then re-evaluate the coupling matrix. Repeat until the dependency graph is a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) with no cycles.
what_it_does: Systematically eliminates circular dependencies or tight coupling between modules.

name: The Dockerfile Optimizer
command: Analyze the current Dockerfile layers. In each step, combine RUN commands, use multi-stage builds, or switch to a lighter base image (like Alpine). Repeat until the image size is reduced significantly and build caching is maximized.
what_it_does: Optimizes Docker builds for speed and minimal image size.

name: The Documentation Sync
command: Compare every comment and docstring against the actual implementation logic. In each iteration, update one outdated description or add missing parameter documentation. Stop once every public method has a docstring that is 100% factually consistent with the code.
what_it_does: Ensures comments and READMEs accurately reflect the current state of the code.

name: The Dry-Run Debugger
command: Mental-trace the provided code line-by-line using a 'state table' you update after every step. Identify one logical discrepancy per iteration, propose a fix, and re-trace the entire flow until the state table matches expected output perfectly. Stop only when a full trace yields zero state errors.
what_it_does: Simulates code execution step-by-step to find logical flaws without running an interpreter.

name: The Error Boundary Guard
command: Identify one plausible runtime failure point in the code, such as a timeout or null return. Inject an error handler or fallback, then immediately scan for the next unhandled failure point. Continue this cycle until every external I/O call and pointer dereference has a defined failure strategy.
what_it_does: Hardens code against edge cases, network failures, and null pointers.

name: The Git History Cleaner
command: Take a large block of staged changes. Identify the smallest logical unit of work within it, suggest a commit message, and 'commit' it mentally. Repeat for the remaining changes until all changes are organized into a sequence of atomic, clear commits.
what_it_does: Break down large changes into clean, atomic, descriptive commits.

name: The Logging Strategic
command: Review the current log statements. In each cycle, either add a 'trace' log to a dark spot in the logic or remove a redundant 'info' log that adds noise. Stop when every error path has a unique log and every happy path has minimal but sufficient observability.
what_it_does: Balances logging to provide visibility without flooding the console.

name: The Memory Leak Detective
command: Examine the lifecycle of the objects in the code. Identify one 'zombie' reference, such as an unremoved event listener or a global closure, and implement a cleanup mechanism. Continue until all allocated resources have a corresponding deallocation or disposal step.
what_it_does: Finds and fixes memory leaks related to closures and event listeners.

name: The Naming Harmonizer
command: Identify a variable or function name that uses 'tech-speak' instead of 'domain-speak' (e.g., 'dataStore' vs 'OrderRepository'). Rename one instance to match the business glossary and update all references. Continue until the code reads like a description of the business process.
what_it_does: Aligns code variable names with the business domain language.

name: The Nano-Optimization Loop
command: Identify a function that is executed thousands of times per second. Look for one micro-optimization (e.g., avoiding object allocation, using bitwise Ops, or caching results). Implement it and re-check the next most expensive operation until further gains are negligible.
what_it_does: Optimizes the 'hot paths' of an application for micro-second gains.

name: The Portability Checker
command: Find a hardcoded string, file path, or URL. Replace it with an environment variable or a configuration hook. Continue this process until the code can run in Local, Staging, and Production environments without a single source code change.
what_it_does: Removes hardcoded values and environment-specific bugs.

name: The README Architect
command: Review the README for a missing critical section (e.g., 'Getting Started', 'Architecture', 'Troubleshooting'). Draft one section per iteration, ensuring it contains actionable steps and code snippets. Stop when a developer totally new to the project can hit 'run' and succeed.
what_it_does: Transforms a basic README into a comprehensive project guide.

name: The Regex Refiner
command: Take an existing 'magic' regex string. Break it down into named capturing groups or separate components with comments explaining each part. Continue until the regex is understandable by a human without using an external tester tool.
what_it_does: Converts complex, unreadable regular expressions into documented, modular versions.

name: The Schema Evolutionist
command: Evaluate a requested database schema change. Break it down into a sequence of backward-compatible steps (e.g., add column, dual-write, then drop old). Review each step for performance impact until a full zero-downtime migration path is established.
what_it_does: Plans database migrations to ensure zero downtime and data integrity.

name: The SQL Turbo-Charger
command: Analyze the query plan for the provided SQL or ORM call. In each step, resolve one performance killer like an N+1 query, a missing index, or a SELECT *. Continue until the query uses indexes for all filters and minimizes data transfer.
what_it_does: Optimizes database queries to eliminate N+1 problems and table scans.

name: The State Machine Enforcer
command: Count the number of boolean flags used to track state (e.g., isLoaded, isError). In each step, consolidate two or more related flags into a single 'status' enum or state machine transition. Repeat until all 'invalid states' (like having two contradicting flags) are impossible by design.
what_it_does: Converts messy boolean flags into a formal state machine.

name: The Test Coverage Hunter
command: Analyze the existing code and describe an execution path not covered by the current tests, specifically looking for boundary conditions. Write a test case for that path, then identify the next most obscure branch. Repeat until all logical branches, including nested catch blocks, are covered.
what_it_does: Generates obscure unit tests by identifying unvisited execution branches.

name: The Test Data Generator
command: Examine the database schema or data model. Generate a mock record that tests a specific edge case (e.g., extremely long name, empty array, negative price). Keep generating unique, diversified data objects until all validation rules have been successfully tested against 'dirty' data.
what_it_does: Creates diverse and realistic mock data for robust testing.

name: The Type-Safety Maximizer
command: Find the weakest type definition in the project (e.g., 'any', 'unknown', or 'Object'). Replace it with a precise interface or union type, then propagate that type through its call stack. Repeat until the code compiles with zero 'any' types and no type assertions.
what_it_does: Removes 'any' types and strengthens TypeScript/static typing.

name: The UX Logic Validator
command: Step through the code as a user navigating a specific feature. In each step, identify a point where the UI might hang, show a confusing state, or miss a loading indicator. Suggest a code-level fix for that UX gap and repeat until the flow is seamless.
what_it_does: Checks code logic against the intended user experience flow.

name: The Vulnerability Scanner
command: Scan the code for data inputs (params, headers, user-input). For each input, attempt to devise a theoretical injection or overflow attack, then implement a specific sanitization or validation fix. Repeat until all data entry points have verified validation logic.
what_it_does: Manually audits code for common security pitfalls like injection or XSS.

name: The 'Hidden Leverage' Uncoverer
command: Evaluate my balance sheet to find assets with high equity but low utilization (e.g., paid-off equipment, intellectual property, receivables). For each iteration, propose a way to leverage that asset for liquidity or growth and calculate the cost of capital. Continue until you have identified three ways to unlock liquid cash without selling assets.
what_it_does: Examines business or personal balance sheets for underutilized assets that could be used as collateral.

name: The Alternative Asset Verifier
command: Examine the private placement memorandum (PPM) or pitch deck provided. For each iteration, find one 'risk factor' that is downplayed or a financial assumption that seems aggressive (e.g., unrealistically low cap rates). Continue until you have produced five critical questions I must ask the sponsor before investing.
what_it_does: Runs a red-flag checklist on private equity, hedge funds, or crowdfunding deals.

name: The Arbitrage Opportunity Finder
command: Compare the pricing of the provided assets across the listed exchanges or marketplaces. For each iteration, identify one discrepancy that exceeds 1.5% after transaction fees and explain the execution steps. Continue searching until you have found three actionable arbitrage opportunities or analyzed all inputs.
what_it_does: Scans price discrepancies between different markets or platforms for specific assets or commodities.

name: The Audit-Trail Creator
command: Identify a large or 'risky' business deduction I am taking. For each iteration, list one specific piece of evidence (receipt, log, contract, photo) that proves the 'ordinary and necessary' nature of the expense. Continue until you have a checklist of 5 items that would satisfy an auditor for that specific deduction.
what_it_does: Constructs a 'defense folder' for a specific deduction to survive an IRS audit.

name: The Beneficiary Checklist
command: Go through my list of financial accounts (401k, IRA, Brokerage, Life Insurance). For each iteration, ask me who the primary and contingent beneficiaries are and flag any account that is currently 'To Estate' or blank. Continue until every account has a secondary backup and a clear distribution path.
what_it_does: Ensures all non-probated assets have correctly assigned beneficiaries to avoid legal battles.

name: The Burn-Rate Runway Extender
command: Take my current monthly burn and cash-on-hand. In each iteration, find a way to cut $5,000 in monthly expenses without reducing headcount, and then recalculate the 'death date'. Continue until the runway is extended by at least 6 months or all non-essential costs are purged.
what_it_does: Identifies non-payroll cuts to extend a startup's life by specific month increments.

name: The Cap-Table Dilution Sieve
command: Take my current cap table and run a hypothetical Series A funding round with specific valuation and liquidation preference terms. In each iteration, increase the option pool or add follow-on investors, then recalculate the founder's exit value at various acquisition prices. Stop once you have mapped the 'breakeven' point where the founder's share becomes less than 10%.
what_it_does: Models the impact of future funding rounds and option pools on founder equity stakes.

name: The Credit Score Optimizer
command: Analyze my credit report data to identify the factors most heavily weighing down my score. In each iteration, suggest the single most impactful action (e.g., pay down specific card, dispute a line) and estimate the resulting point increase. Stop once the projected score reaches 800 or all negative factors have a mitigation plan.
what_it_does: Reverse-engineers credit reports to find the most efficient path to a 800+ score.

name: The Crypto-to-Fiat Exit Map
command: Analyze my cryptocurrency holdings and the target fiat amount I want to withdraw. For each iteration, identify an exchange with the highest liquidity for a specific pair and calculate the expected slippage and withdrawal fees. Continue until you have a step-by-step 'liquidation ladder' that minimizes total costs and addresses potential bank freezes.
what_it_does: Plans the liquidity steps to move large crypto positions into bank accounts with minimal slippage and tax.

name: The Debt Interest Decelerator
command: Input my various debts, interest rates, and minimum payments. For each iteration, compare the 'Avalanche' vs. 'Snowball' methods and calculate the total interest paid. Suggest one 'debt consolidation' or '0% balance transfer' move that further reduces the timeline. Stop when the interest-paid-to-zero is minimized to its absolute lowest possible value.
what_it_does: Calculates the most mathematically efficient path to zero debt across multiple high-interest accounts.

name: The Dividend Snowball Builder
command: Analyze my current dividend-paying holdings and monthly budget. In each iteration, reinvest the dividends back into the highest-yield-on-cost positions and incorporate a monthly contribution. Continue simulating year-by-year until the monthly dividend income exceeds the monthly expenses for three consecutive years.
what_it_does: Calculates the exact point where portfolio dividends cover all living expenses.

name: The Employee Stock Option (ESO) Mapper
command: Use my strike price, current share value, and number of options. For each iteration, simulate an exit price ($10, $50, $100) and calculate the AMT tax liability for the year of exercise. Continue until you have a 'tax-efficient exercise schedule' that spreads the cost over multiple years.
what_it_does: Calculates the tax hit and profit of exercising stock options under different scenarios.

name: The Expense Category Tightener
command: Examine my profit and loss statement line by line. For each iteration, suggest one specific way to reduce that expense category by 15% through negotiation, substitution, or elimination. Keep iterating until the total projected net profit increases by at least 10%.
what_it_does: Systematically questions every line item in a P&L statement to find 10% bottom-line improvement.

name: The Fee-Aversion Auditor
command: Examine the provided investment fund documentation to extract every hidden fee, load, and internal expense ratio. After each discovery, propose a lower-cost index fund alternative and calculate the difference in wealth over 20 years. Continue until no further fee reductions can be found or the total expense ratio is below 0.10%.
what_it_does: Deep-dives into 401k/brokerage prospectuses to find hidden expense ratios and administrative fees.

name: The High-Yield Cash Sifter
command: Scan current rates for HYSA, Money Market Funds, and T-Bill ETFs. For each iteration, calculate the 'after-tax yield' based on my provided tax bracket (considering state-tax exemptions for T-bills). Continue until you find the highest net-yield option that allows for 2-day liquidity.
what_it_does: Finds the best-yielding, liquid place for your 'emergency fund' right now.

name: The Insurance Gap Finder
command: Review my list of current insurance policies and my personal/business asset list. In each iteration, invent a 'black swan' event (e.g., professional negligence, flood in a non-flood zone) and check if I am covered. Continue until you have identified three major coverage gaps and recommend the specific policy type to fix them.
what_it_does: Searches for catastrophic scenarios that are not covered by your current insurance stack.

name: The Mortgage Refi-Calculator
command: Input my current mortgage terms and the new potential rate. In each iteration, include an additional closing cost (appraisal, title, points) and calculate how many months it takes to reach the 'break-even' point. Continue until the analysis accounts for all possible fees and the resulting monthly savings are net-positive within 24 months.
what_it_does: Determines the exact break-even point for refinancing a loan including all closing costs.

name: The Phantom Subscription Purge
command: Review my transaction history to identify recurring payments, even those with varying amounts or intervals. For each iteration, highlight one non-essential or overlapping service and calculate the 5-year opportunity cost if that money were invested at 7%. Keep searching until you have identified $200 in monthly savings or scanned the entire statement.
what_it_does: Identifies recurring micro-transactions, duplicate services, and zombie subscriptions in bank statements to reclaim lost cash.

name: The Portfolio Stress-Tester
command: Apply a specific historical macroeconomic shock (e.g., 1970s stagflation, 2008 crash) to my current asset allocation and calculate the drawdown. After each simulation, suggest one specific hedge or rebalance to mitigate that specific loss, then rerun the simulation. Continue until the portfolio maintains a positive beta-adjusted return across four different catastrophic scenarios.
what_it_does: Simulates various historical and hypothetical economic disasters to find hidden weaknesses in an investment portfolio.

name: The Real Estate Cap Rate Auditor
command: Take the seller's Pro Forma for this property. In each iteration, increase the vacancy rate by 2% and the maintenance reserve by 3%, then recalculate the Net Operating Income (NOI) and Cap Rate. Continue until you find the 'maximum purchase price' where the property still cash-flows at a 7% yield.
what_it_does: Pressure-tests a real estate deal by adjusting vacancy and repair assumptions.

name: The Rule of 72 Visualizer
command: Start with my current principal and expected return. In each iteration, change one variable (add $100/mo, increase return by 1%, or reduce taxes) and calculate the new time-to-double. Stop when the 'doubling time' is reduced by at least speed of 3 years compared to the starting point.
what_it_does: Iteratively adjusts variables to see how to double your money faster.

name: The Subscription Stack Optimizer
command: List all my monthly subscriptions and their prices. For each iteration, ask me to rate the 'criticality' (1-10) and then calculate the Value Index (Criticality / Cost). Sort the list and recommend cancelling the bottom three. Stop once the total monthly cost is reduced by 20%.
what_it_does: Ranks SaaS or personal tools by 'Utility per Dollar' to find which to cancel first.

name: The Tax Nexus Hunter
command: Analyze my business activity logs for potential economic nexus triggers or permanent establishment risks in different regions. For each iteration, identify one specific tax obligation I might be unaware of, calculate the potential liability, and then refine your search for the next most likely risk. Continue this cycle until you have found five distinct exposure points or exhausted the data provided.
what_it_does: Scrapes provided business activities to identify unrecognized tax liabilities across different jurisdictions and tax treaties.

name: The Tax-Loss Harvest Guide
command: Review my current open investment positions and their cost basis compared to today's price. For each iteration, identify a net-loss position and suggest a 'substantially identical' replacement that avoids the wash-sale rule. Stop once you have found enough losses to offset my provided capital gains or until all positions are checked.
what_it_does: Identifies losing positions in a brokerage account that should be sold to offset capital gains.

name: The Unit Economics Stressor
command: Analyze my Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) and Lifetime Value (LTV) data. In each iteration, increase the CAC by 10% and decrease the retention rate by 5%, then recalculate the payback period. Continue iterating until the business model becomes unsustainable, then suggest the 'safety threshold' for marketing spend.
what_it_does: Breaks down a product's profitability to find the point where scaling actually loses money.

name: The Vendor Contract Negotiator
command: Analyze my provided vendor contract and current market rates for similar services. For each iteration, generate one specific negotiation script or 'threat to churn' based on a competitor's offer. Continue until you have identified a path to a 15% discount or more favorable payment terms.
what_it_does: Finds leverage points and competitor pricing to force vendor discounts.

name: The Wash-Sale Watchdog
command: Look at my recent 'Sell' transactions and my 'Watchlist' for buying. For each iteration, check if a purchase would trigger a wash-sale based on a sale within the last 30 days. Recommend a 'cooling off' date for each ticker. Continue until all planned trades are screened for tax-compliance.
what_it_does: Prevents accidental tax penalties when buying and selling similar stocks within 30 days.

name: The Yield Curve Interpreter
command: Check the current 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. For each iteration, look at the delta between them over the last 6 months and correlate it with historical recession leads. Suggest one specific sector rotation (e.g., move to consumer staples) if the curve is inverting further. Stop once a full defensive plan is outlined.
what_it_does: Analyzes Treasury spreads to predict recession timing and suggest defensive moves.

name: The Cold Outreach Sniper
command: Review this prospect's recent LinkedIn post or company news and find a non-obvious connection to my service. Draft a 3-sentence email: one sentence on their news, one on the problem it creates, and one on how I solve it. Iterate until the email feels like a 1-to-1 message that couldn't possibly be a template.
what_it_does: Customizes cold emails based on a prospect's recent activity or news.

name: The Expert Positioning Audit
command: Scan my profile or proposal for 'permission-seeking' language like 'I hope to', 'I can try', or 'just checking'. Replace these with 'authoritative' verbs and diagnostic questions that position me as an expert. Keep iterating until the entire text sounds like a doctor prescribing a solution rather than a vendor begging for a gig.
what_it_does: Removes 'freelancer' language and replaces it with 'consultant' or 'partner' terminology.

name: The Ghost-Proof Follow-up
command: Draft a sequence of five follow-up emails, where each iteration provides a new relevant resource or insight rather than asking for an update. Keep refining the 'ask' in each email to make it a one-word answer for the client. Stop when you have a 5-step sequence that feels helpful and never desperate.
what_it_does: Generates a sequence of low-friction follow-up emails that provide value instead of checking in.

name: The Late-Payer Gentle Nudge
command: Draft four versions of an overdue invoice reminder, increasing the 'firmness' and mentioning late fees in the final two. After each version, check if the language remains objective and non-emotional. Stop once you have a progression that preserves the relationship early on but protects your cash flow late in the cycle.
what_it_does: Automates the escalation of late payment reminders from friendly to firm.

name: The Objection Pre-emptor
command: Adopt the persona of a cynical, budget-conscious CEO and find three reasons to reject this proposal in every iteration. After each critique, revise the proposal to address those specific concerns directly within the text. Repeat this cycle until you can no longer find a logical reason for the client to say 'no'.
what_it_does: Simulates a skeptical client to find and fix weak points in your proposal.

name: The Onboarding Streamliner
command: Create an onboarding checklist for this specific service that includes every piece of access, information, and asset I need from the client. Group them into a 'Single Request' email that minimizes back-and-forth. Iterate until the client can provide everything in under 10 minutes, making me look exceptionally organized.
what_it_does: Automates the repetitive parts of starting a new project to look ultra-pro.

name: The Panic-to-Pivot Response
command: Draft an apology for [mistake] that takes 100% responsibility without making excuses. Immediately pivot the draft to three possible 'make-good' options or an accelerated timeline for the fix. Keep iterating until the tone is humble but the focus is entirely on the resolution and future-proofing.
what_it_does: Handles a major mistake or missed deadline by proposing a solution before the client reacts.

name: The Portfolio Narrative-Maker
command: Input the technical tasks I did for this project and weave them into a 'Hero's Journey' narrative. Focus on the obstacle the client faced and the specific 'insight' I used to solve it. Keep refining until the case study proves I can think strategically, not just execute tasks.
what_it_does: Turns a boring list of tasks into a compelling story of problem-solving.

name: The Quarterly Business Review
command: Compile the wins from the last 90 days of this retainer into a 'Value Realized' report. Quantify the time saved, money made, or stress reduced for the client. Iterate the report until it clearly demonstrates that my fee is an investment with a positive return, ending with a 'Vision for next quarter' section.
what_it_does: Generates a 'value report' for long-term clients to show why they should keep paying you.

name: The Scope Creep Guard
command: Analyze our current task list for words like 'ongoing', 'assist', or 'improve' and replace them with measurable milestones. Continue identifying potential 'feature creep' entry points until every deliverable has a clear definition of done and a specific exclusion list. Keep iterating until no sentence can be interpreted in more than one way.
what_it_does: Hardens a project scope by identifying vague verbs and replacing them with binary deliverables.

name: The Testimonial polisher
command: Take this raw client feedback and identify the 'before' state, the 'action' taken, and the 'after' result. Rewrite it into a 2-sentence testimonial that highlights a specific metric or transformation. Keep iterating until the quote sounds natural but specifically addresses a major pain point of my target audience.
what_it_does: Turns a vague 'they were great' client quote into a high-impact case study snippet.

name: The Up-sell Architect
command: Look at the current project deliverables and list three problems the client will have once this work is finished. For each problem, draft a concise 'Phase 2' pitch that solves it. Keep refining these pitches until they feel like a necessary continuation of the current success rather than an extra cost.
what_it_does: Identifies the 'next logical problem' the client will face and drafts an add-on proposal.

name: The Value-Based Pivot
command: Rewrite this service proposal to remove all mentions of 'hours' or 'time', replacing them with the financial or strategic impact on the client's business. Iterate through the draft, calculating the potential ROI for each feature until the proposed price feels like a small fraction of the value. Stop once the proposal focuses 100% on results and 0% on labor.
what_it_does: Converts hourly-based service descriptions into high-ticket, outcome-focused value propositions.

name: The Zero-Meeting Filter
command: Analyze the purpose of this proposed meeting and determine what info needs to be exchanged. Draft a proposal to handle the update via a recorded video or shared doc instead. Keep refining the message until it provides so much clarity that the client agrees the meeting is no longer necessary, or provides a strictly timed agenda.
what_it_does: Converts a scheduled meeting into an asynchronous update or a specific agenda.

name: The Adverse Event Anticipator
command: For the prescribed treatment, list the five most dangerous early warning signs a patient might experience. Iteratively refine this list by adding specific, sensory-based descriptions (e.g., 'not just pain, but a tearing sensation') that a layperson can easily identify. Continue until each warning sign has a clear 'if this, then do that' action step attached.
what_it_does: Generates a personalized 'what to look for' list for patients starting high-risk treatments.

name: The Barrier Breaker
command: Review the proposed treatment plan and identify three likely socioeconomic barriers the patient will face (e.g., transportation, cost, refrigeration). After identifying them, propose a logistical workaround for each, then critique those workarounds for practical feasibility. Repeat the refinement until the plan is realistic for a patient with limited resources.
what_it_does: Identifies Social Determinants of Health (SDOH) obstacles in a treatment plan and iterates solutions.

name: The Billing Code Auditor
command: Analyze the clinical note and suggest the most specific ICD-10 codes possible. For each iteration, identify missing documentation elements (like acuity, site specificity, or causal links) needed to reach a higher specificity code. Continue prompting for these details until the note supports the most granular codes available for the diagnosis.
what_it_does: Refines clinical documentation to ensure the highest specific ICD-10 codes are supported.

name: The Chart Auditor
command: Read this patient note from the perspective of a malpractice attorney or an insurance auditor. Iteratively identify areas where the rationale for a treatment or 'refusal of care' is weakly documented. For each weakness, suggest specific phrasing to strengthen the 'medical necessity' and 'clinical judgment' narrative until the note is legally and administratively bulletproof.
what_it_does: Scrutinizes notes from a medicolegal perspective to ensure 'medical necessity' is undeniable.

name: The Culture-Sensitive Clinician
command: Examine the proposed care plan through the lens of [Specific Culture/Religion: e.g., Orthodox Judaism, Hmong traditions]. Iteratively identify potential points of friction—such as fasting requirements, modesty concerns, or traditional medicine interactions. Suggest modifications for each point until the plan is culturally congruent and respectful without compromising clinical outcomes.
what_it_does: Evaluates care plans for potential cultural friction or dietary/religious conflicts.

name: The Differential Destabilizer
command: Review the patient case and present the most likely diagnosis, then immediately argue against it by identifying three 'can't-miss' rare mimics. Iterate by refining the symptom weightings and proposing one new diagnostic test for each mimic, continuing until no plausible alternative explanations remain for the specific constellation of labs and symptoms. After each iteration, re-rank the differentials based on the new evidence path.
what_it_does: Systematically challenges a primary diagnosis with rare mimics and atypical presentations until exhaustive.

name: The Empathetic Reframer
command: Take the clinical facts provided and draft a script for delivering this news to a family. Iteratively refine the script using the SPIKES protocol, checking after each version if the tone is sufficiently empathetic and whether it allows for patient silence and emotional processing. Stop only when the script balances clinical honesty with profound psychological support.
what_it_does: Transforms blunt medical news into compassionate, patient-centered dialogue using the SPIKES protocol.

name: The Guideline Guardian
command: Compare this patient's treatment plan against the latest [Specific Organization, e.g., GOLD] guidelines. Iteratively flag any deviations from the 'Grade A' recommendations and ask for a clinical justification for the deviation. Continue until the plan either aligns perfectly with guidelines or contains a documented, evidence-based reason for bypassing them.
what_it_does: Cross-references a proposed treatment plan against current Gold Standard clinical guidelines (e.g., AHA, ADA).

name: The Handoff Harmonizer
command: Take the patient's current chart and organize it into the I-PASS (Illness severity, Patient summary, Action list, Situation awareness, Synthesis by receiver) format. Iteratively refine the 'Action List' to ensure every item is time-bound and assigned, and verify the 'Situation Awareness' includes clear 'if-then' contingency plans. Stop when the handoff is concise enough to be read in under 90 seconds.
what_it_does: Converts complex patient histories into the structured I-PASS format for safe shift changes.

name: The Health Literacy Translator
command: Take the provided clinical discharge summary and rewrite it for a patient with no medical background. After each draft, run a Flesch-Kincaid analysis and simplify any jargon or sentence structures that exceed a 5th-grade level. Repeat this process until the text is perfectly clear, actionable, and free of passive voice, finishing only when the reading level goal is met.
what_it_does: Iteratively simplifies complex medical instructions until they meet a Grade 5 reading level and cultural relevance.

name: The Interaction Interrogator
command: Analyze the provided medication list and identify potential 'prescribing cascades' where one drug is likely treating a side effect of another. For each iteration, suggest one medication to taper or discontinue and predict the resulting symptom change, continuing until the list represents the minimum effective therapeutic burden. Provide a cited rationale for each proposed removal.
what_it_does: Scans medication lists for cascade effects where drugs are treating side effects of other drugs.

name: The Lab Trend Analyst
command: Examine the chronological lab values provided and calculate the rate of change for key markers. In each iteration, identify which markers are trending toward the limit of normal even if currently within range, and suggest a possible underlying mechanism. Continue until you have a list of three early-intervention opportunities based on these trajectories.
what_it_does: Analyzes longitudinal lab data to identify subtle physiological shifts before they hit 'abnormal' ranges.

name: The Mechanism Explainer
command: Explain the Mechanism of Action (MOA) for this medication using a non-medical metaphor. Iteratively refine the metaphor to ensure it accurately reflects the biological process without using words like 'molecule', 'receptor', or 'inhibition'. Continue until the explanation is so visual that a child could draw it, while preserving the core therapeutic concept.
what_it_does: Creates visceral, metaphorical explanations of how a drug works for patient buy-in.

name: The Motive Interviewer
command: Assume the role of a clinician using Motivational Interviewing. Iteratively respond to the patient's 'change talk' and 'sustain talk' using the OARS (Open-ended questions, Affirmations, Reflective listening, Summaries) framework. Continue the dialogue loop, focusing on amplifying the patient's own reasons for change, until a specific, patient-led goal is established.
what_it_does: Simulates a Motivational Interviewing conversation to help patients overcome ambivalence.

name: The PICO Researcher
command: Take this vague clinical query and turn it into a structured PICO (Patient, Intervention, Comparison, Outcome) question. Iteratively narrow the population and intervention parameters to ensure the question is searchable. Once the PICO is sharp, suggest the top three databases and specific mesh terms to find the most relevant randomized controlled trials.
what_it_does: Refines clinical questions into the PICO format and identifies the best databases for answers.

name: The SOAP Note Shrinker
command: Read this raw transcript and extract a full SOAP note, then iteratively remove every word that doesn't contribute to clinical decision-making or billing compliance. In each cycle, identify 'fluff' phrases and replace them with standard medical abbreviations or precise clinical terms. Stop when the note is at its most concise possible state while still justifying the selected billing code.
what_it_does: Compresses rambling patient encounters into high-信号-density SOAP notes without losing clinical meaning.

name: The Symptom Cluster Finder
command: Look at this disparate list of symptoms and labs. Iteratively try to group them into known clinical syndromes or 'triads'. After each grouping attempt, perform a 'gap analysis' to see which symptoms remain unexplained, and search for a larger unifying diagnosis until every single patient complaint is accounted for by the proposed pathophysiology.
what_it_does: Groups disparate symptoms into recognized or emerging clinical syndromes.

name: The Triage Triage-er
command: Review this list of incoming patient complaints and vitals. Iteratively rank them from highest to lowest acuity, explaining the physiological 'why' behind each ranking. After each sort, simulate a resource constraint (e.g., 'only one bed available') and justify who gets seen first until the logic for the entire queue is airtight and ethically sound.
what_it_does: Rapidly sorts a list of patient complaints based on physiological stability and resource intensity.

name: The Boredom Buster Loop
command: Name three toys my child hasn't played with in a month; I will suggest a 'setup' or 'invitation to play' combining them in a novel way. After you set it up, report the engagement level, and I will refine the next suggestion until the child is independently playing for 20 minutes.
what_it_does: Reintroduces 'old' toys in new creative ways to stop the cycle of buying new ones.

name: The Budget Leak Plugger
command: Review my list of recurring bills and subscriptions, selecting the highest one to research a specific lower-cost competitor or negotiation script. Provide the action step, wait for my report on the savings achieved, and move to the next highest bill until total monthly expenses decrease by 15%.
what_it_does: Audits recurring household expenses and negotiates or finds alternatives to reduce monthly burn.

name: The Bulk Buy Optimizer
command: Input a bulk item price and quantity; I will compare it to standard grocery sizes and factor in your available storage space. Recommend 'Buy' or 'Skip', then ask for the next item, continuing until your shopping list is optimized for both budget and pantry capacity.
what_it_does: Calculates true price-per-unit versus storage cost to optimize warehouse club shopping.

name: The Capsule Wardrobe Curate
command: Analyze my current clothing list and identify the one item that fits the fewest outfits; suggest a way to style it or mark it for donation. After each decision, reassess the remaining items for color harmony, continuing until I have a 30-piece collection where every top matches every bottom.
what_it_does: Iteratively eliminates clothing to build a highly versatile, minimal color-coordinated wardrobe.

name: The Chore Equity Engine
command: List all current household tasks and their estimated mental load scores; propose a trade between family members to balance the total effort. Adjust the distribution based on my feedback regarding 'felt fairness' and repeat the balancing act until all residents agree the workload is equitable.
what_it_does: Redistributes household labor based on time-on-task and mental load rather than just task count.

name: The Deep Clean Cycle
command: Focus on one specific sub-zone of the room I name and provide three rapid-fire cleaning steps to complete in 15 minutes. Ask for a photo description or confirmation of completion, then move to the adjacent sub-zone, continuing until the entire room meets a 'guest-ready' standard.
what_it_does: Breaks a messy room into 15-minute bursts, iterating until high-standard cleanliness is reached.

name: The Emergency Fund Accelerator
command: Ask me for my total spending today and identify one 'impulse' or 'convenience' cost to eliminate tomorrow. Calculate the projected savings, update my emergency fund progress, and repeat daily until the fund reaches its $1,000 (or specified) target.
what_it_does: Finds micro-savings opportunities daily to reach a specific cash reserve goal faster.

name: The Family Meeting Architect
command: Ask each family member (through me) for their 'peak' and 'pit' of the week; then identify one logistics conflict and one appreciation to share. Compile these into a 15-minute agenda and refine it based on my feedback until the meeting flow feels supportive and efficient.
what_it_does: Iteratively builds an agenda for a weekly family meeting that addresses everyone's needs.

name: The Frictionless Morning Loop
command: Analyze my current morning schedule and identify one specific bottleneck causing a delay; propose a physical staging solution (e.g., shoe placement) and ask for the result of a one-day trial. Continue suggesting micro-adjustments until the transition from 'wake up' to 'out the door' is under 45 minutes.
what_it_does: Refines a family morning routine by identifying and eliminating specific seconds of delay.

name: The Gift-Giving Strategist
command: Ask me to recall one interest or 'off-hand comment' made by a family member this week; translate that into three specific gift options at different price points. Store the winner in a digital 'gift vault' and repeat for the next person until every immediate family member has two 'reserved' ideas.
what_it_does: Maintains a rolling list of meaningful gift ideas based on observations, avoiding last-minute buys.

name: The Guest-Ready Rapid Response
command: Focus on the entryway: give me two 2-minute tasks to clear the visual clutter; then move to the living room seating area. Suggest one sensory 'reset' (scent or lighting) and continue through the bathroom until 30 minutes have elapsed or the 'public path' is clear.
what_it_does: An accelerated 30-minute loop to make the public areas of a home presentable for visitors.

name: The Holiday Stress Defragmenter
command: Identify one holiday goal (e.g., 'mailed cards') and break it into four micro-tasks spread across the next four weeks; then ask for my feedback on the feasibility. Adjust the timeline and add the next goal's tasks, continuing until the entire holiday schedule is plotted with no more than two tasks per week.
what_it_does: Back-maps holiday preparations to prevent the last-minute December panic.

name: The Home Maintenance Sentry
command: Direct me to one specific area of the house (e.g., under the kitchen sink) to check for a specific maintenance issue; provide a 2-minute fix or a 'clear' status. Move to the next most critical system, repeating until all high-risk household components have been inspected and logged.
what_it_does: Systematically checks home systems (filters, batteries, leaks) to prevent expensive repairs.

name: The Home Office Ergonomics Loop
command: Ask me where I feel tension after a day of work; suggest one specific adjustment to my chair, monitor, or lighting. After I make the change, wait for a 4-hour 'work block' report, then refine or move to the next discomfort until I can work for 6 hours pain-free.
what_it_does: Iteratively adjusts your home workspace to reduce physical pain and increase focus.

name: The Kid-Logic Conflict Resolver
command: Describe the current behavior and the suspected trigger; I will provide a 'connection-first' script for you to try. Report back the child's reaction, and I will refine the language or approach in the next iteration until the child moves from a reactive state to a cooperative state.
what_it_does: Generates and tests de-escalation scripts based on the specific emotional trigger of the child.

name: The Legacy Recipe Digitizer
command: Ask me for one step of a family recipe that uses imprecise measurements (e.g., 'until it looks right'); help me define a measurable metric for that step. We will iterate through each instruction until the recipe is fully quantified and ready to be successfully shared or doubled.
what_it_does: Translates vague 'a pinch of this' family recipes into standardized, scalable instructions.

name: The Meal Prep Efficiency Loop
command: Take my three chosen recipes and create an interleaved cooking schedule that uses shared appliances efficiently. After I complete the first block of tasks, adjust the remaining schedule based on my actual pace, continuing until all meals are stored and the kitchen is clean.
what_it_does: Optimizes kitchen workflow to minimize active cooking time and cleanup during Sunday prep.

name: The Nutrient Density Auditor
command: Tell me a meal you plan to cook; I will suggest one 'micro-upgrade' (e.g., adding spinach to sauce) that doesn't significantly change the flavor. If you accept, I'll suggest one more swap for a different macronutrient, continuing until the meal reaches a 'high-density' rating without family pushback.
what_it_does: Improves the nutritional value of a standard family meal through iterative ingredient swaps.

name: The Pantry Puzzle Solver
command: Evaluate my current ingredient list and suggest one meal; if I confirm I have the staples, suggest the next, continuing until all perishable items are utilized. After each meal plan, update the remaining inventory and stop once the fridge is empty of short-dated goods.
what_it_does: Iteratively designs meal plans using only existing expiring ingredients to reach zero-waste goals.

name: The Picky Eater Bridge
command: Identify one 'safe' food my child eats and propose a 'bridge food' that shares one sensory characteristic (texture or color) but introduces a new nutrient. Suggest a preparation method, wait for the 'taste test' result, and keep iterating on slight variations until the new food is accepted three times.
what_it_does: Gradually introduces new foods by iterating on flavor profiles the child already likes.

name: The School-Form Automator
command: Upload or describe the contents of a school flyer; I will extract dates, requirements, and costs into a structured table. Confirm these are added to your calendar, then ask for the next document, repeating until your physical 'to-process' pile is empty.
what_it_does: Processes the weekly 'folder dump' of school papers into a digital calendar and task list.

name: The Screen Time Negotiator
command: Ask for the child's requested screen time duration; propose a 'price' in terms of household tasks or outdoor play minutes. Adjust the ratio based on my feedback of their current behavior, repeating the negotiation until a daily 'tech contract' is finalized and signed.
what_it_does: Creates a tiered system where screen time is earned through specific offline contributions.

name: The Seasonal Wardrobe Switch
command: For the incoming season, ask me to check one category of clothing (e.g., winter coats) for fit and damage; then suggest which items from the outgoing season should be laundered and stored. Continue this category-by-category audit until the entire wardrobe is swapped and the 'out-of-season' bin is sealed.
what_it_does: Manages the transition of clothing between seasons while auditing for fit and wear.

name: The Sentimentality Sorter
command: Describe a sentimental object you're struggling to discard; I will prompt you to tell its story and then ask if a photo and that story are sufficient to honor the memory. We will iterate through one box of memories at a time until only the most essential physical items remain.
what_it_does: Helps declutter sentimental items by creating digital records and deciding on physical retention.

name: ELI5 Then Expert Loop
command: Explain your previous answer twice. First, as you would to a smart 12-year-old, with concrete analogies and zero jargon. Second, as you would to a working expert in the field, with precise terminology, edge cases, and at least one citation. End with one sentence that bridges them.
what_it_does: Pairs an intuitive explanation with a rigorous one so you understand and can defend the concept.

name: Socratic Loop
command: Do not give me the answer. Instead, ask me one question at a time, building on my previous response, until I have reasoned my way to the answer myself. Stop after eight questions or when I say "done".
what_it_does: Replaces an answer with a coaching dialogue — strongest for learning, decision-coaching, and therapy-style reflection.

name: Teach-Back Loop
command: I am going to explain {CONCEPT} back to you. Listen, then (1) score my explanation 1-10 on accuracy and 1-10 on completeness, (2) name the single most important thing I got wrong or missed, (3) ask me the one question that would prove I really understand it.
what_it_does: Inverts the usual flow — you teach, the model grades and probes — which cements learning faster than asking for an explanation.

name: The Clause Ancestry Tracker
command: Take a modified clause and compare it against the original 'gold standard' version in the library. Identify what was lost in the edit and propose a way to keep the new context without losing the original protection. Repeat for every redlined section in the document.
what_it_does: Traces a clause back to its original intent and ensures new edits don't break it.

name: The Clause Connector
command: Verify that Clause A does not contradict Clause B, then move to Clause C and check it against all previous clauses. Document any circular dependencies or conflicting definitions and propose fixes. Stop only when every section has been cross-referenced against every other section with zero found contradictions.
what_it_does: Checks for internal contradictions and logical loops across different document sections.

name: The Data Privacy Shredder
command: Look at each data collection requirement in this policy and argue why it is unnecessary for the core business function. Remove or restrict the clause unless I provide a counter-argument, then move to the next data point. Stop when the document represents the most aggressive 'privacy by design' stance possible.
what_it_does: Minimizes data collection clauses to reach the absolute 'minimum viable data' limit.

name: The Definitions Auditor
command: List every capitalized term and verify it has a corresponding definition. Then, check every definition to ensure the term is actually used in the body of the contract. Flag unused definitions for removal and missing definitions for creation, continuing until the list is perfectly synchronized.
what_it_does: Ensures every capitalized term is defined and every defined term is used correctly.

name: The Edge-Case Evolver
command: Present a bizarre edge-case scenario that would break this policy, then draft an amendment to cover it. Generate the next, even more complex scenario based on the updated text. Keep iterating this flow until the policy can handle a 'Black Swan' event without ambiguity.
what_it_does: Force-tests a policy by generating increasingly unlikely but legally significant edge cases.

name: The Enforcement Simulator
command: Act as a conservative judge and interpret the most ambiguous sentence in this document. Suggest a more precise replacement to prevent judicial activism, then find the next most ambiguous phrase. Continue until you, as the judge, find no room for 'creative interpretation' in the entire text.
what_it_does: Predicts how a judge would interpret specific language based on recent case law.

name: The Ethics Alignment Loop
command: Analyze this policy against the company's stated ESG goals. Find one area where the legal language is compliant but ethically 'weak,' and suggest a higher-standard alternative. Continue searching for these 'ethical gaps' until the policy perfectly reflects the brand's values.
what_it_does: Reviews policies for ESG and ethical compliance beyond just the legal minimums.

name: The Incentive Mapper
command: Identify a clause that might encourage 'gaming the system' or bad behavior by either party. Propose a counter-measure in the text, then look for the next behavioral loophole. Continue until you have mapped and mitigated the five most likely perverse incentives in the agreement.
what_it_does: Analyzes how contract terms will drive human behavior, adjusting for perverse incentives.

name: The Jurisdiction Shifter
command: Analyze this contract under Delaware law, identify a conflict, and rewrite it for compliance. Then, shift the analysis to English law, then California law, correcting the text at each step. Continue this cross-border refinement until the document is harmonized for all three specified jurisdictions.
what_it_does: Rewrites a document to comply with a new legal jurisdiction while highlighting conflicts.

name: The Mutual Benefit Balancer
command: Identify the most one-sided clause in this draft and rewrite it to be 'market-standard' fair for both parties without losing protection. Ask me if the balance is acceptable; if not, suggest an alternative and move to the next most biased clause. Stop when every clause has achieved a neutral, win-win stance.
what_it_does: Adjusts contract language to ensure mutual incentives while maintaining core protections.

name: The Poison Pill Hunter
command: Analyze this contract as if you are a hostile litigator trying to bankrupt the user’s company. Identify one subtle trap, propose a defensive rewrite, and then re-scan the entire document for the next trap. Continue this cycle until you can no longer find a single phrase that can be maliciously misinterpreted.
what_it_does: Scans agreements for hidden liability traps by role-playing a hostile litigator looking for loopholes.

name: The Redline Reducer
command: Identify the clause most likely to be redlined by a standard procurement department. Provide three levels of compromise (Conservative, Mid, Aggressive) and integrate the 'Mid' version into the text. Repeat for the top 10 most contentious clauses until the document is 'pre-negotiated' for rapid signing.
what_it_does: Predicts common counter-party objections and incorporates 'pre-accepted' compromises.

name: The Regulatory Echo
command: Rewrite the current policy using the specific 'regulatory tone' of the SEC (or chosen agency). Identify any missing mandatory keywords and insert them where appropriate. Repeat this process for each section until the document sounds like it was written by the regulator themselves.
what_it_does: Aligns internal policies with the specific tone and vocabulary of specific governing bodies.

name: The Regulatory Stress-Tester
command: Select a random regulation from the GDPR, CCPA, or DMA and simulate a specific compliance failure against this policy. Propose a mitigation, then immediately select a new regulation and repeat. Continue until the policy survives five consecutive diverse regulatory stress tests without needing a revision.
what_it_does: Evaluates internal policies against shifting global regulations through randomized adversarial scenarios.

name: The Signature-Ready Polisher
command: Check all page numbering and table of contents links for accuracy. Then, verify that all signature blocks match the parties named in the preamble. Finally, ensure all 'Exhibit' references point to existing attachments. Iterate until every formal element is 100% verified for execution.
what_it_does: A multi-step final check for formatting, cross-references, and signature blocks.

name: The Third-Party Risk Sieve
command: Scan the vendor contract for 'indemnity' and 'liability' flow-throughs. For each risk found, draft a corresponding requirement for the vendor to provide proof of insurance or security audits. Continue through all sections until all downstream risks have a documented mitigation strategy in the text.
what_it_does: Sifts through vendor contracts to extract and mitigate downstream risks to the main firm.

name: The 'Explain Like I'm Five' Brand Loop
command: Take this technical product description and rewrite it for a non-technical CEO. After each draft, identify the most complex word remaining and replace it with a simple analogy, repeating until the entire value proposition can be understood by a 5th grader without losing the professional tone.
what_it_does: Simplifies complex B2B tech value propositions until they are crystal clear.

name: The Ad Refresh Decay Loop
command: Identify the core 'big idea' of this winning ad and brainstorm five radical visual pivots that keep the same messaging but look completely different. Iteratively refine these concepts until you have three distinct aesthetic variations ready to combat creative fatigue.
what_it_does: Generates visual and copy pivots for winning ads that have started to decline.

name: The Ad Scent Consistency Loop
command: Compare the ad creative with the landing page and the checkout page; identify every visual or tonal discrepancy. Iteratively adjust the copy and imagery on all three to ensure a unified 'scent trail,' stopping only when the journey feels like one continuous experience.
what_it_does: Ensures visual and textual consistency from the first ad click to the final checkout.

name: The B2B Cold Loom Script Loop
