command: Research [Prospect Name]'s company and find one specific UI/UX flaw or marketing gap they have. Write a 45-second script for a screen-share video that briefly shows the flaw and hints at the solution, iteratively cutting the 'intro' until the value is shown in the first 5 seconds.
what_it_does: Writes 60-second personalized video scripts for high-value sales prospects.

name: The Brand-Voice Mirror Loop
command: Analyze the provided speech samples and rewrite this boring draft in that exact cadence, using their specific idioms and sentence structures. After each draft, measure the 'personality score' against the source and iterate until the AI version is indistinguishable from the provided samples.
what_it_does: Forces content to match a specific founder's quirky tone through recursive refinement.

name: The Case Study Story-Arc Loop
command: Rewrite this client success story using the 'Hero’s Journey' framework, emphasizing the 'Rock Bottom' moment before they used our product. Iteratively heighten the stakes and the specific ROI metrics until the case study feels like a transformation story rather than a corporate report.
what_it_does: Polishes dry case studies into high-stakes narrative hero journeys.

name: The Comparison Table Sharpener
command: Identify three features where we beat [Competitor] and two where we are roughly equal. Iteratively draft a comparison table that highlights our strengths as 'Essential Modern Features' and their strengths as 'Legacy Bloat,' refining the language until our product looks like the clear future-proof choice.
what_it_does: Creates biased (but fair-seeming) comparison tables for competitive positioning.

name: The FAQ-into-Sales Loop
command: Take these 10 standard FAQs and rewrite each answer as a mini-sales pitch that highlights a unique feature of the product. Iteratively refine the 'Question' to reflect real customer concerns while ensuring the 'Answer' leads directly toward the 'Buy' button.
what_it_does: Turns boring FAQ sections into persuasive content that handles objections.

name: The Hook-Pattern Interrupter
command: Draft five variations of the opening hook for this video ad using different psychological triggers like 'curiosity gap' or 'negative social proof.' Test each against the target audience persona, discard the weakest three, and iterate on the winners until the hooks feel impossible to scroll past.
what_it_does: Iterates on the first 3 seconds of ad creative to maximize thumb-stop rates.

name: The Influencer Seeding Pitch Loop
command: Draft a DM to this influencer that mentions a specific niche detail from their last post (non-sponsored). Iteratively refine the offer to send them free product 'with no strings attached,' adjusting the tone to be less 'marketer' and more 'genuine fan' until the pitch feels impossible to ignore.
what_it_does: Crafts no-pressure gift offers to influencers that actually get a 'yes'.

name: The Lead Magnet Optimization Loop
command: Analyze our current lead magnet and identify the 'Time to Vitamin' (how long it takes for the user to get a win). Iteratively simplify the delivery and front-load the most actionable tip until the user can get a result within 5 minutes of downloading.
what_it_does: Refines the 'Opt-in' incentive to ensure it solves a high-value problem immediately.

name: The Local SEO Dominance Loop
command: Analyze the top-rated local competitors in this city and list their mentioned services and neighborhoods. Iteratively weave these local landmarks and specific service niches into our description until the content feels hyper-local while maintaining a professional national brand voice.
what_it_does: Optimizes GMB descriptions and local landing pages for 'Near Me' dominance.

name: The Micro-Influencer Briefing Loop
command: Compare this creator brief against these 5 successful user-generated content examples and identify where the instructions are too restrictive or too vague. Keep loosening the brand guidelines and tightening the 'required outcomes' until the brief allows for creativity while guaranteeing the core value proposition is mentioned clearly.
what_it_does: Refines creator briefs to ensure brand safety while maintaining authentic creator expression.

name: The Moral Outrage Hook Loop
command: Identify a common best practice in this industry and write a thesis on why it's actually harmful or outdated. Iteratively sharpen the 'villain' of the story and the 'new hero' solution until the hook is provocative enough to stop a scroll but logical enough to defend in the comments.
what_it_does: Develops 'spicy' points of view for thought leadership that spark civil debate.

name: The Negative Space Search Loop
command: Analyze these top 10 SERP competitors and their bidding activity; identify semantic clusters they pay for but fail to explain depthfully in their blog. Iterate by drafting a 200-word seed for each gap, checking against current search results, and repeating until 5 unique 'zero-competition' topics are identified.
what_it_does: Finds high-intent keywords that competitors are bidding on but not covering in their organic content.

name: The Newsletter Retain-or-Drain Loop
command: Review the last three editions of this newsletter and generate a subject line for the next one that creates a loop from the previous content. Refine the preview text iteratively until it offers a 'micro-win' that makes opening the email feel mandatory, stopping only when the curiosity gap is peaked.
what_it_does: Iterates on newsletter subject lines and snippets to maximize open rates and minimize unsubscribes.

name: The Objection-Crusher Funnel Loop
command: Scan this landing page copy and generate one cynical 'but what if...' objection for every paragraph. Rewrite the section to preemptively answer the objection without losing momentum, then repeat the scan until you can no longer find a reasonable reason for a prospect to hesitate.
what_it_does: Systematically identifies and counters customer hesitation points across a multi-stage landing page.

name: The PASCAL Sales Loop
command: Apply the PASCAL framework to this product description by first expanding the ‘Agitation’ phase to 3 paragraphs of deep empathy for the user's pain. Iteratively refine the transition to the 'Solution' until it feels like the only logical exit from the pain described, stopping only when the CTA feels like a relief.
what_it_does: Uses Problem-Agitation-Solution-Case Study-Action-Limit to build sales pages.

name: The Price-Value Paradox Loop
command: List the core product features and brainstorm 10 'bonus' digital assets that cost us $0 to fulfill but solve a specific user problem. Iteratively pair these bonuses with the core offer and refine the 'Total Value' versus 'Price' comparison until the offer feels like a 'no-brainer' steal.
what_it_does: Increases the perceived value of an offer without changing the actual product.

name: The Reddit-to-Content Alchemy Loop
command: Search provided subreddit threads for complaints about [Competitor X] and list the top 5 pain points mentioned. Iteratively draft a 'Us vs. X' comparison page focused solely on these 5 points, refining the 'Better Alternative' section until it sounds like a relief to the frustrated users.
what_it_does: Turns subreddit complaints into high-converting product comparison pages.

name: The Search Intent Alignment Loop
command: Analyze the top-ranking results for [Keyword] and determine if the intent is informational, commercial, or navigational. Rewrite our page headers and meta-tags to match that specific intent profile, iterating until the 'satisfaction score' for a first-time searcher would be 10/10.
what_it_does: Ensures landing page content matches the specific psychology of a search query.

name: The SMS Marketing Urgency Loop
command: Rewrite this promotional offer into 140 characters or less for SMS. Iteratively remove every word that isn't a direct benefit or a clear call-to-action, until the message provides a 'heartbeat' of urgency without feeling like spam.
what_it_does: Drafts ultra-short mobile messages that drive immediate action without being annoying.

name: The Testimonial Sculpting Loop
command: Review this 30-minute interview transcript and pull out every sentence where the customer mentions a specific dollar amount or time saved. Iteratively rewrite these as punchy 1-sentence testimonials, keeping the customer's unique voice but removing 'ums' and filler until they are ready for a hero section.
what_it_does: Extracts the most powerful 'money quotes' from raw customer interview transcripts.

name: The Trojan Horse Guest Post Loop
command: Draft a pitch to this specific editor that solves a problem they recently tweeted about, weaving our product into the solution organically. Adjust the 'self-promotion level' down by 10% each iteration until the pitch reads like a helpful tip from a peer rather than a PR blast.
what_it_does: Refines guest post pitches to look like genuine, selfless value adds while subtly positioning a brand.

name: The Upsell Path Refiner
command: Analyze the main product and brainstorm three 'logical next steps' or complementary accessories. Draft a post-purchase upsell offer for each, iteratively refining the 'one-time offer' discount and the 'speed of implementation' benefit until the take-rate predicted is over 15%.
what_it_does: Creates post-purchase sequences that increase Average Order Value (AOV).

name: The Viral Thread Hook Loop
command: Generate 10 different 'Opening Hook' styles for this topic (e.g., The Statistic, The Contradiction, The Personal Failure). Rank them by their 'curiosity gap' and refine the top 2 until they are so compelling that not clicking 'Show more' feels like missing a secret.
what_it_does: A/B tests 10 different ways to open a social media thread for maximum virality.

name: The Webinar Registration Hook Loop
command: Identify the one 'Secret' or 'Framework' that will be revealed in the webinar. Iteratively rewrite the headline and three bullets to focus entirely on the 'transformation' the attendee will experience, stopping only when the 'FOMO' of missing the secret is a 9/10.
what_it_does: Optimizes the registration page to maximize sign-up rates for live events.

name: The Zero-Click Content Loop
command: Take this 1,500-word article and condense it into a series of 5 value-dense LinkedIn slides or a Twitter thread. Continually remove 'fluff' and 'read more' links, focusing on delivering the full utility within the platform, until the post stands alone as a complete guide.
what_it_does: Transforms long-form blog posts into platform-native threads that satisfy the algorithm.

name: The Automation Prospector
command: Ask me about my repetitive digital tasks for 5 minutes. For each task I describe, suggest either a No-Code tool (Zapier/Make), a script (Python), or a built-in feature to automate it. Continue until we have a list of five 'Quick Wins' that save at least 30 minutes a week.
what_it_does: Scans your manual repetitious tasks for automation opportunities.

name: The Burnout Brake
command: Look at my current project load and score my risk of burnout from 1-10 based on our discussion. For every high-stress project, identify a 'maintenance' task that can be paused or deferred. Repeat this until my estimated 'Daily Stress Load' falls back into a sustainable green zone.
what_it_does: Audits your workload for sustainability and forces restorative rest.

name: The Calendar Ghost Purge
command: Review my upcoming weekly calendar and for every entry, challenge its necessity by asking 'What happens if we cancel this?'. Keep iterating through each event, suggesting either a cancellation, a reduction in duration, or an asynchronous alternative. Stop once we have reclaimed at least 20% of the total weekly time.
what_it_does: Reviews a calendar to audit and remove low-value meetings and 'zombie' standing blocks.

name: The Clarity Prompt
command: Review my to-do list for 'vague' items like 'Plan trip' or 'Do research'. For every vague item, redefine it as a single, physical action starting with a verb (e.g., 'Google 3 hotels in London'). Iterate until every task on the list is so clear that a stranger could do it.
what_it_does: Refines vague tasks into 'stale-proof' actionable next steps.

name: The Context Switch Killer
command: Take my random list of to-dos and group them by 'Context' (e.g., @laptop, @phone, @errands, @deep-thinking). Reorder the list so tasks within the same context are performed back-to-back. Continue until the daily plan has the minimum possible number of context transitions.
what_it_does: Groups disparate tasks by 'context' to reduce mental reload time.

name: The Deep Work Timer
command: Analyze my project list and estimate the 'Cognitive Intensity' of each project from 1-10. Design a schedule that clusters all 8-10 level tasks together for a morning deep work block, followed by 1-3 level tasks. Continue adjusting the schedule until no high-intensity tasks are placed in 'fatigue zones'.
what_it_does: Engineers a perfect 'Focus Day' based on your specific cognitive load.

name: The Delegation Architect
command: Take a task I do regularly and document it step-by-step as a Standard Operating Procedure. After each step, identify potential points of confusion a beginner might have and clarify them. Repeat until the process is so simple that a virtual assistant could execute it perfectly without further input from me.
what_it_does: Converts complex tasks into clear SOPs for others to execute.

name: The Dependency Unblocker
command: Break down my project into individual steps and identify which tasks are currently blocked by others. For every blocked task, propose one immediate action to 'unstick' it or a workaround to bypass the dependency. Continue until every task has a clear, unblocked path to completion.
what_it_does: Visualizes and solves for hidden bottlenecks in a complex project.

name: The Distraction Dissector
command: Take my list of recent distractions and categorize each by 'Internal' (thought-based) or 'External' (environment-based). For every item, propose a design-level change (like a browser blocker or phone placement) that removes the temptation entirely. Continue until we have a preventative measure for every distraction on the list.
what_it_does: Finds the root cause of recurring distractions and suggests engineering-level fixes.

name: The Energy Mapper
command: Evaluate my task list against my self-reported energy peaks throughout the day. Rearrange the schedule to place deep, creative work in high-energy slots and administrative 'shallow' work in low-energy troughs. Repeat this adjustment until every high-priority task is matched with a peak energy window.
what_it_does: Aligns your hardest tasks with your peak biological energy windows.

name: The Focus Sphere Audit
command: List my 5 core life values and my top 20 time-expenditures from last week. For each expenditure, calculate the alignment percentage with one of the core values. Iterate through the list, suggesting where to cut time from low-alignment activities and move it to high-alignment ones until the budget is balanced.
what_it_does: Evaluates if your time spent matches your stated life values.

name: The Frog Swallower
command: Identify the one task on my list that I have been procrastinating on the most. Break it down into 5-minute 'micro-tasks' that feel impossibly easy to complete. Keep breaking it down until the first five steps require zero cognitive resistance to start.
what_it_does: Identifies the most dreaded task and breaks it into non-intimidating sub-steps.

name: The Habit Stacker
command: List my currently established daily 'anchor' habits (e.g., brushing teeth, making coffee). For every new habit I want to form, find the most logical anchor to 'stack' it onto. Continue refining the pairings until the sequence feels natural and the friction to start is minimal.
what_it_does: Attaches new desired habits to existing daily anchors.

name: The Inbox Zero Mechanic
command: Start with my first 10 emails/files and force me to make a final decision: Archive, Do Now, or Snooze with a specific date. If I hesitate, suggest the most logical action based on personal history. Repeat this for the next batch of 10 until the view is empty.
what_it_does: Applies a 'one-touch' rule to clear a digital mess systematically.

name: The Information Dieter
command: Check my list of recurring information sources (newsletters, podcasts, feeds). For each source, ask 'What was the last actionable thing I learned from this?' and if the answer is none, mark it for 'Unsubscribe'. Continue through the entire list until only high-impact signals remain.
what_it_does: Cuts out low-value content consumption to reclaim mental bandwidth.

name: The Minimalist Review
command: Read my project plan and identify the most 'bloated' features or tasks that don't directly contribute to the primary goal. Systematically suggest things to cut or postpone until we have a lean, 1-week execution plan. Stop when the plan contains only the essential steps to prove the concept.
what_it_does: Strips a project plan down to its absolute Minimum Viable Success criteria.

name: The Paperwork Shredder
command: List all my tiny '2-minute' admin tasks that have been lingering. Organize them into a 'Sprint' and prompt me for the status of the first one. After I confirm completion, immediately prompt for the next one, continuing until all 'micro-tasks' are cleared from the board.
what_it_does: Blasts through low-level administrative chores in a timed sprint.

name: The Priority Pivot
command: Given the new information/change I just provided, re-evaluate my entire current priority list. Identify which 3 items are no longer relevant and which 1 item must now move to the top. Continue adjusting the list until the new priority order perfectly reflects the updated reality.
what_it_does: Re-aligns your focus when a major external change occurs.

name: The Shutdown Ritualist
command: Review my current end-of-day habits and identify 'open loops' that cause me stress at night. Iteratively build a 15-minute shutdown ritual that addresses each loop, clears the desk, and sets the intention for tomorrow. Continue refining the sequence until it covers all my work-life transition triggers.
what_it_does: Drafts a personalized end-of-day sequence to ensure mental disconnection.

name: The Triage Razor
command: Take my provided list of tasks and categorize the first item as 'Delete', 'Delegate', or 'Must Do Today' based on my stated goals. Continue this process for every single item, justifying the 'Must Do' label against my focus, until the list is fully exhausted. After each 5 items, summarize what we've removed to show progress.
what_it_does: Aggressively filters an inbox list by forcing high-stakes trade-offs until it is empty.

name: The Weekly Reflector
command: Summarize my last 7 days based on my logs and tell me what the biggest 'time leak' was. Then, suggest one specific change to my schedule for next week to prevent that leak. Keep iterating through my weekly wins and losses until we have a 'Game Plan' for an improved next week.
what_it_does: Analyzes the past week's wins and losses to improve the next week.

name: The API First-Principles Loop
command: Describe the core data entities for this feature and their relationships. Iteratively refactor the schema to reduce redundancy, handle 10x the current data volume, and maintain simple, intuitive endpoint names. Stop when the API documentation is clean enough that an external developer could build a third-party app on it without asking you questions.
what_it_does: Ensures your product's underlying data structures are scalable and developer-friendly.

name: The Competitive Ghost-Writer
command: Analyze our top three competitors and draft the press release they would write to 'neutralize' our upcoming launch. In the next iteration, modify our product roadmap to include a 'moat' feature that makes their hypothetical response irrelevant. Continue this competitive chess match for three turns until our launch strategy is robust against immediate counter-moves.
what_it_does: Drafts how your competitors will respond to your next feature launch.

name: The Customer Insight Distiller
command: Scan the provided interview transcripts and extract every explicit pain point into a structured table. In the next iteration, cross-reference these with existing feature requests to find gaps, then generate a prototype solution for each gap. Continue refining the solution descriptions until they are specific enough for a designer to wireframe without further clarification.
what_it_does: Converts raw interview transcripts into high-fidelity, actionable feature requests and pain points.

name: The Dependency Disentangler
command: List all dependencies between the features in the next sprint, then brainstorming one 'mock' or 'interface' approach to break each hard dependency. Iteratively redraw the sequence of work to maximize parallelization across the 3 sub-teams. Stop when the critical path is reduced to its shortest possible duration and no team is 'waiting' on another.
what_it_does: Finds ways to decouple features so teams can work in parallel without blocking.

name: The Edge Case Exorcist
command: Evaluate every functional requirement in this PRD and draft 3 'what-if' failure scenarios for each, then update the requirements to address them. Continue this process iteratively, widening the scope to include network latency, concurrent user collisions, and API outages, until no single point of failure remains unmitigated. Stop when you have a 100% complete error-handling section for every feature.
what_it_does: Systematically identifies and handles obscure failure modes in your PRD requirements.

name: The Feature De-Scoper
command: Take this feature list and identify the one 'core action' that delivers 80% of the value. For every other sub-feature, provide a justification for why it can't be moved to V2. Keep stripping away functionality until removing one more thing would make the product literally unusable for the primary persona. Stop when you have a true 'Day 1' release scope.
what_it_does: Aggressively strips features down to their absolute Minimum Viable Product state.

name: The Friction Hunter
command: Walk through this user journey step-by-step and count the number of decisions the user must make at every screen. In each iteration, suggest one way to automate a decision or combine two steps into one. Keep iterating until the 'Time to Value' is reduced by at least 40% from the starting point.
what_it_does: Identifies every unnecessary click or cognitive load step in a user flow.

name: The GTM Resonance Loop
command: Draft a Go-To-Market headline for each feature in this launch plan, then simulate a critique from a cynical target persona. Adjust the messaging to address their specific skepticism, then increase the specificity of the value proposition in the next loop. Stop only when the messaging feels impossible for the target persona to ignore based on their stated frustrations.
what_it_does: Aligns product features with marketing messaging through recursive persona-benefit matching.

name: The Hypotheses-Driven Builder
command: Take every feature in this roadmap and rewrite it as a hypothesis: 'We believe [feature] will result in [outcome] for [persona]. We will know this is true when we see [metric] change by [percentage].' Iteratively adjust the success metrics to be more sensitive and harder to fake. Stop when every feature has a statistically sound experiment design attached to it.
what_it_does: Converts product ideas into scientific experiments with clear pass/fail criteria.

name: The Jobs-to-be-Done Validator
command: For every feature in this spec, write a 'Job Story' (When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]). If a feature doesn't clearly solve a job or provides a weak outcome, mark it for deletion or redesign. Keep refining the job stories until every single line item in the PRD is directly tied to a high-priority user need.
what_it_does: Ensures every proposed feature maps directly to a fundamental user 'job' and desired outcome.

name: The Legal & Privacy Gauntlet
command: Examine every data point this feature collects and classify its necessity and privacy risk. For every 'High Risk' item, propose an anonymized or local-only alternative. Repeat the review until the product achieves 'Privacy by Design,' requiring the minimum possible personal data to function. Stop when a compliance officer would have zero major objections.
what_it_does: Reviews product requirements against GDPR, CCPA, and general ethical data practices.

name: The Localization & Culture Check
command: Review this UI copy for idioms, cultural references, or length issues that will break in translation (e.g., German or Japanese). In each loop, replace one complex English phrase with a simpler, globally understood concept. Continue until the interface uses only 'International English' and the layout allows for 30% text expansion in every label.
what_it_does: Reviews product UI and copy for potential issues in international markets.

name: The Metric Cascader
command: Start with the North Star metric and break it down into three supporting 'input' metrics. For each input metric, identify the specific user actions (events) that must be tracked to measure it accurately. Continue refining the event list until it serves as a complete implementation ticket for an analytics engineer, with zero ambiguity on what to track.
what_it_does: Connects high-level business goals to granular product telemetry events.

name: The Narrative Roadmap Synthesizer
command: Take these 10 roadmap items and group them into 3 'themes', then write a one-paragraph story about how a user's life is better after these are shipped. Iteratively refine the story to sound less like a feature list and more like a visionary transformation. Stop when the narrative is inspiring enough to be the centerpiece of a company all-hands meeting.
what_it_does: Turns a dry list of features into a compelling vision story for the company.

name: The OKR Stress-Tester
command: Analyze my current OKRs and flag any that are 'tasks' rather than 'outcomes', then rewrite them to be quantifiable. For every Key Result, play devil's advocate and explain how a team could 'game' the metric without actually delivering value. Repeat the refinement until every KR has a counter-metric that prevents low-quality speed and is 100% objective.
what_it_does: Iteratively critiques and tightens Key Results to ensure they are outcome-based and measurable.

name: The Post-Launch Retrospective Loop
command: Analyze these notes from the last sprint's retrospective and categorize every complaint into 'Process', 'People', or 'Tools'. For every problem, generate two 'Countermeasures' and one way to measure if the countermeasure is working next sprint. Iterate on these actions until every major complaint has a clear owner and a deadline for implementation.
what_it_does: Converts vague retrospective complaints into concrete process improvements.

name: The Premortem Paradox
command: Assume it is one year from now and this product launch has been a total disaster. List every technical, market, and organizational reason why it failed, then work backward to create a mitigation task for every reason. Repeat this until your launch plan includes a specific 'circuit breaker' for every high-risk failure point identified.
what_it_does: Forces you to envision product failure to prevent it from happening.

name: The Prioritization Pressure-Cooker
command: Take my list of features and assign each a RICE score, then immediately delete the bottom 25% and redistribute their resources to the top 10%. Repeat this cycle of evaluation and aggressive cutting until only the three most impactful, non-negotiable items remain. After each cut, justify why the remaining items are now more critical for the upcoming quarter.
what_it_does: Forces trade-offs between roadmap items using a recursive RICE-based elimination strategy.

name: The Stakeholder Shield
command: Act as a skeptical CEO, CFO, and CTO reviewing this product strategy, and ask the three hardest questions each would have. Draft my responses, then critique those responses for lack of data or strategic misalignment. Keep iterating on the responses until you can answer every objection with a data-backed, confident rebuttal that aligns with company vision.
what_it_does: Simulates difficult questions from leadership to prepare you for roadmap presentations.

name: The Technical Debt Auditor
command: Review this roadmap and identify the 'technical shortcuts' required to hit each deadline, then calculate the 'interest' (future work) each creates. For each item, propose a 'middle path' that reduces debt while maintaining 80% of the speed. Continue adjusting the roadmap until the total projected debt for the quarter is below your specified threshold.
what_it_does: Balances new feature development against the long-term cost of technical shortcuts.

name: The Tiered Pricing Stress-Test
command: Allocate all current and upcoming features into 'Free', 'Pro', and 'Enterprise' tiers, then identify the 'fences' that force a user to upgrade. Iteratively move one feature between tiers and simulate the impact on churn vs. expansion revenue. Stop when you have found a configuration that maximizes upgrade incentives without making the 'Free' tier feel broken or useless.
what_it_does: Optimizes feature placement across pricing tiers to maximize Average Revenue Per User (ARPU).

name: The User Onboarding Accelerator
command: Identify the primary 'Aha!' moment for this product and map every screen a user sees before reaching it. In each iteration, remove one barrier, form field, or tooltip that doesn't directly lead to that moment. Continue until the path to value is under 60 seconds of active user effort. Stop when the flow is a straight line to success.
what_it_does: Optimizes the time it takes for a new user to reach their first 'Aha!' moment.

name: The Comps Narrative Architect
command: Review the provided comparable properties and draft a narrative explaining why the subject property is superior or fairly priced. After each draft, look for logical gaps or 'stretches' in the comparison and tighten the reasoning. Stop when the narrative provides an undeniable justification for the target price point.
what_it_does: Frames disparate CMA data into a cohesive story that justifies a specific listing price.

name: The Developer Pitch Deck Loop
command: Review raw parcel data and zoning info to draft a 'Highest and Best Use' summary. Iteratively add data points about local demand, exit values, and potential density. Critique the draft: does it speak the language of IRR and 'build-to-rent'? Stop when you have a 1-page executive summary that a developer would use to secure funding.
what_it_does: Polishes raw land or teardown data into a high-level development proposal.

name: The Emotional Listing Pivot
command: Identify five technical specs of the property and translate them into emotional benefits (e.g., 'granite counters' becomes 'the stage for your holiday dinners'). Iteratively blend these into a 200-word story, checking after each version if the 'feeling' outweighs the 'feature.' Continue until the text evokes a physical sense of 'belonging' in the reader.
what_it_does: Rewrites technical property descriptions into narrative journeys that trigger emotional 'home' feelings.

name: The Expired Listing Re-Framer
command: Examine the previous, failed listing for this property. Identify three 'points of failure' (photos, price, description, marketing). For each point, draft a superior alternative that a seller would find compelling. Continue refining the 'New Strategy' pitch until it sounds like a rescue mission that makes the previous agent look amateur.
what_it_does: Analyzes why a listing failed and rewrites the strategy to win the new listing.

name: The Feature-Benefit Stress Test
command: List a property feature, then ask 'So what?'. Answer that question, then ask 'So what?' again to find the deeper meaning. Continue this loop for every major room in the house until every feature is linked to a specific lifestyle outcome. Stop once you have a 'Lifestyle List' that is 100% focused on the buyer's future.
what_it_does: Challenges every property feature with the 'So What?' question to find real selling points.

name: The Ghost-Lead Re-Activator
command: Write a 9-word email designed to elicit a response from an inactive buyer. Critique the draft for 'salesiness' and rewrite to increase genuine curiosity or direct helpfulness. Keep iterating until you have three variations that each feel impossible to ignore but take under 10 seconds to read.
what_it_does: Drafts and refines high-curiosity email sequences to wake up unresponsive leads.

name: The Hyper-Local Insight Engine
command: Take raw data points about schools, transit, and amenities to draft a 'neighborhood vibe' paragraph. Evaluate if the draft sounds like a generic brochure, then inject specific local landmarks and 'insider' knowledge cues. Continue refining until the text sounds like a 10-year resident wrote it.
what_it_does: Synthesizes raw neighborhood data into punchy 'lifestyle' snippets for marketing collateral.

name: The Inspection Report Translator
command: Take a list of red-flag items from an inspection report and rewrite them to focus on the solution rather than the problem. After each draft, check if the tone remains professional yet calming for a nervous buyer. Iterate until the list feels like a productive 'to-do' sequence rather than a reason to kill the deal.
what_it_does: Turns scary-sounding house inspection flags into manageable, non-alarming action items.

name: The Listing Heat-Map Optimizer
command: Analyze the current listing text, identify the 'hook' and 'utility' features, then rewrite to maximize perceived value. After each rewrite, assign a 'conversion score' from 1-100 based on psychological triggers; continue iterating until the score exceeds 95. Stop once the flow feels natural but the urgency is palpable.
what_it_does: Iteratively critiques a listing description to emphasize high-value features based on current buyer psychology trends.

name: The Luxury-Tier Upgrader
command: Review the current property description and identify 'common' real estate jargon (e.g., 'nice,' 'spacious'). Replace these with 'legacy-building' and 'architectural' terminology. After each pass, check for pretentiousness—keep it sophisticated but accessible. Stop when the text feels like it belongs in a high-end design magazine.
what_it_does: Takes standard real estate copy and elevates the vocabulary for a high-net-worth audience.

name: The Off-Market Hunter Script
command: Draft a letter to a homeowner who isn't listing, expressing interest on behalf of a buyer. After each draft, remove one 'professional' word and replace it with a 'casual' word to lower the recipient's guard. Repeat until the letter reads like a handwritten note from a friendly neighbor while maintaining legal clarity.
what_it_does: Refines direct-to-seller scripts to sound like a neighbor rather than a solicitor.

name: The Open House Social Hype
command: Draft an Instagram Story sequence for an open house: the 'Mystery,' the 'Reveal,' and the 'Urgency.' Critique each slide—is there a clear reason to visit in person rather than just looking at photos? Continue iterating until the 'Call to Action' is irresistible. Stop when you have an 8-slide storyboard that builds a logical emotional arc.
what_it_does: Engineers a series of Teaser/Day-Of/Recap social media posts that build FOMO.

name: The Price-Drop Narrative Builder
command: Draft a public-facing announcement for a price reduction. Iterate to remove phrases like 'motivated seller' or 'priced to sell' and replace them with logic-based hooks like 'Immediate Equity Play' or 'New Market Positioning.' Continue until the price drop feels like a reward for the first person to see it.
what_it_does: Frames a price reduction not as a failure, but as a strategic opportunity for buyers.

name: The Referral Loop Generator
command: Draft a 30-day post-close check-in message that asks for a referral without being cringey. Analyze the draft: is it 80% value and 20% 'the ask'? Keep adjusting the ratio and the 'call to help' until it feels like a natural conversation between friends. Stop when you have a three-step sequence that feels helpful, not promotional.
what_it_does: Optimizes a post-close communication sequence to maximize the likelihood of a referral.

name: The Short-Term Rental Math Plotter
command: Take the property data and estimate potential STR (Airbnb) revenue based on local nightly rates. After each calculation, find one 'value-add' (e.g., hot tub, themed room) that could boost the ADR and re-run the numbers. Stop iterating when you have a 'Bronze, Silver, Gold' investment strategy that shows three clear ROI paths for the buyer.
what_it_does: Iteratively calculates and refines the 'investor narrative' for potential STR properties.

name: The Virtual Tour Scriptwriter
command: Draft a 60-second video script for a property tour. After each draft, read it aloud (simulated) and cut any word that feels stiff or 'salesy.' Focus on 'the life lived here' rather than the 'items for sale.' Stop when the script flows perfectly for a handheld iPhone walkthrough with zero pauses.
what_it_does: Creates a conversational, high-energy video script for property walkthroughs.

name: The Zillow-Proof Hook
command: Write the first 140 characters of this listing description. After each version, simulate the 'scroll-past' test: does this instantly differentiate from 10 other similar homes? Iterate by swapping generic adjectives for high-contrast verbs or shocking unique selling points. Stop only when the first sentence creates an unavoidable 'open loop' in the reader's mind.
what_it_does: Refines the first 140 characters of a listing to stop the scroll on mobile apps.

name: Chain-of-Verification
command: Take your previous answer. Step 1: list every factual claim you made as a numbered list. Step 2: for each claim, write the single best verification question. Step 3: answer each verification question independently from scratch. Step 4: rewrite the original answer, removing or correcting any claim that failed verification.
what_it_does: Reduces hallucinations by decomposing an answer into atomic claims and re-verifying each one independently.

name: Five Whys Loop
command: Take the issue I just described. Ask "why" five times in sequence, each layer probing the cause of the previous answer. Then synthesize: what is the root cause, what surface-level fixes won't solve it, and what one intervention would.
what_it_does: Drives from symptom to root cause using the classic Toyota technique.

name: The Skeptic Loop
command: You are now in Skeptic mode. For the prior response, list the three weakest assumptions, the evidence that would falsify each, and one concrete way the conclusion could be wrong. Be specific, name sources, and avoid hedging.
what_it_does: Forces the model to stress-test its own answer by surfacing weak assumptions and counter-evidence.

name: Research Deep Dive
command: Go three layers deep on the topic above. Layer 1: the consensus view, with sources. Layer 2: the credible minority view that disagrees with the consensus, with sources. Layer 3: the one frontier question researchers are actively fighting about right now. End with the single paper or essay I should read first.
what_it_does: Forces depth instead of a Wikipedia-style summary.

name: The Abstract Compression Loop
command: Take this abstract and reduce its word count by exactly 10% without losing any significant findings or methodological details. After each reduction, identify the most important remaining sentence and re-verify that the 'So What?' of the research is still prominent. Stop only when the text is under 150 words and every word is load-bearing.
what_it_does: Shrinks an abstract by 10% in each iteration while retaining all key results and implications.

name: The Acronym & Variable Consistency Checker
command: Extract a list of all acronyms and mathematical variables used in the text. In each iteration, check a different 5-page segment of the paper to ensure these terms are never redefined or used differently. Flag any inconsistencies immediately for correction. Stop when the entire document uses a unified nomenclature without a single deviation.
what_it_does: Ensures every variable name and acronym is used identically throughout 50+ pages.

name: The Adversarial Citation Stress-Test
command: Extract all factual claims from my draft and search for credible contradictory evidence for each. In each iteration, present a counter-argument to a specific claim and force me to revise or strengthen the evidence until no rebuttals remain unaddressed. Stop when every core assertion has successfully withstood three rounds of bad-faith interrogation.
what_it_does: Systematically attempts to debunk each cited claim until all sources prove logically resilient to counter-evidence.

name: The Argumentative Weight Balancer
command: Count the number of sentences dedicated to each of my three core arguments. If one argument is significantly 'lighter' than the others, suggest three new points or pieces of evidence to expand it. Repeat this balancing act until each core pillar of the thesis is roughly equal in persuasive weight and depth of evidence.
what_it_does: Adjusts the proportional space given to different arguments to match their relative importance.

name: The Citational Diversity Auditor
command: Analyze my bibliography and estimate the demographic and institutional diversity of the authors (University types, global regions, gender). In each iteration, suggest two scholars from underrepresented regions or backgrounds who have published relevant work on this topic. Stop when the bibliography reflects a global perspective rather than a localized bubble.
what_it_does: Evaluates a bibliography for geographic, gender, and institutional variety in sources.

name: The Counter-Intuitive Trend Finder
command: Observe the provided dataset or summary. Identify one outlier or result that contradicts the 'expected' trend. In each iteration, propose a radical hypothesis for why this anomaly exists and suggest a way to test it. Stop when you have identified three 'anomalous insights' that could lead to a breakthrough discovery.
what_it_does: Scours datasets or literature for anomalies that contradict the prevailing narrative.

name: The Future Direction Generator
command: Based on the findings of this paper, identify three logical 'next steps' for the field. In each iteration, push one step further: 'If that study succeeds, then what?' Continue this extrapolation until you have a roadmap for a five-year research program. Stop when you have a list of three distinct, high-impact grant ideas for the future.
what_it_does: Extrapolates current results to predict the next five years of research in the field.

name: The Hypothesis Stress-Generator
command: Take my working hypothesis and generate three distinct 'black swan' scenarios that would falsify it. In each iteration, refine the hypothesis to account for these scenarios or develop a methodological control to rule them out. Stop when the hypothesis is robust enough to include explicit 'falsifiability' criteria for future researchers.
what_it_does: Attempts to design experiments that would prove your own hypothesis wrong.

name: The Literature Map Builder
command: Identify three core authors in this field. For each, summarize their stance on [Topic], then find another author who disagrees with them. Create a table mapping these 'intellectual tensions.' Continue adding authors until you have a map of four distinct 'schools of thought' and where they overlap. Stop when the landscape of the debate is fully charted.
what_it_does: Builds a conceptual map of how authors disagree and agree on a specific topic.

name: The Methodology Gap Hunter
command: Review the provided list of studies and tabulate the variables studied against the variables ignored. Continue identifying 'blind spots' in the research landscape, such as underrepresented populations or overlooked temporal factors, until you have proposed five viable directions for a novel study. Stop when each gap is backed by a specific void in the existing literature.
what_it_does: Scans literature abstracts to identify missing demographic, geographic, or procedural variables in current research trends.

name: The Methodology Stress-Test
command: Critically analyze my proposed research methodology. In each iteration, identify one source of bias (e.g., recall bias, social desirability) or a logistical failure point and challenge me to fix it. Continue until we have identified five major risks and added specific 'mitigation strategies' to the method section. Stop when the design is maximally robust.
what_it_does: Acts as a cynical reviewer finding every possible way your study design could fail.

name: The Missing Citation Scout
command: Scan the document for every empirical claim that lacks a specific citation. For each claim, suggest the type of study that would support it and search for the most-cited paper matching that description. Continue until every contestable statement is pinned to a specific, high-impact reference.
what_it_does: Identifies orphaned claims that lack a citation and suggests the most likely authoritative source.

name: The Narrative Thread Stitcher
command: Read the paper one paragraph at a time. After each paragraph, state how it directly supports the central thesis and suggest a transition sentence that leads naturally to the next point. Continue this audit until every paragraph has a clear 'bridge' and the logical flow from introduction to conclusion is unbreakable.
what_it_does: Ensures every paragraph in a long paper explicitly connects to the primary research question.

name: The Non-Expert Reality Check
command: Summarize the final conclusion of this paper in one sentence. Now, imagine you are a professional in [Target Field, e.g., a high school teacher]. Tell me why this conclusion is hard to apply to your daily work. Refine the explanation until the teacher understands exactly what to change in their classroom based on your research. Stop when the 'application' is unmistakable.
what_it_does: Tests if a research conclusion is explained clearly enough to be useful to its end-users.

name: The Policy Implication Extractor
command: Review these research findings and identify a specific stakeholder group (e.g., local government, NGOs). In each iteration, draft one policy recommendation based on the data and anticipate a practical barrier to its implementation. Refine the recommendation to address that barrier until you have three turnkey policies that are both evidence-based and politically feasible.
what_it_does: Translates academic findings into three actionable policy recommendations for specific stakeholders.

name: The Recursive Glossary Refiner
command: Select a highly technical term from the text and define it using only the thousand most common English words. After each definition, ask a hypothetical layperson for clarification, then refine the definition to remove ambiguity while keeping scientific accuracy. Repeat until the concept is crystal clear to a 10th-grade level without losing its technical essence.
what_it_does: Iteratively simplifies technical definitions while maintaining precision until a non-specialist understands the core mechanics.

name: The Semantic Pivot Searcher
command: Take my current research question and identify how three unrelated disciplines (e.g., Economics, Biology, Sociology) would phrase it. For each discipline, generate a set of niche keywords and search strings, then refine based on the relevance of the results found. Continue until you have a cross-disciplinary bibliography that covers the topic from five distinct angles.
what_it_does: Explores adjacent academic fields by translating search queries into different disciplinary terminologies.

name: The Source Pedigree Tracer
command: Identify the primary citation for the core concept in this text. Look up that citation, find its primary source, and repeat the process until you reach the foundational paper or theory that first established the idea. For each step, summarize how the concept evolved from the original to the current usage. Stop when you reach a source published before 1990 or a seminal textbook.
what_it_does: Follows the chain of citations backward to find the seminal 'origin' paper of a specific concept.

name: The Statistical Flaw Detector
command: Examine the 'Results' section and identify potential statistical pitfalls like p-hacking, small sample sizes, or selection bias. In each iteration, focus on one specific table or figure and suggest a sensitivity analysis that could challenge the reported significance. Stop when you have provided a 'limitations' paragraph that addresses the three most likely threats to internal validity.
what_it_does: Critiques the statistical power and potential biases in a paper's results section.

name: The Taxonomy Architect
command: Analyze these raw qualitative data points and propose an initial categorization scheme. In each step, introduce three new data points and attempt to fit them into the schema, modifying the categories or adding sub-categories as necessary to maintain perfect mutual exclusivity. Stop when 50 consecutive data points can be categorized without altering the structure.
what_it_does: Categorizes disparate data points into a hierarchical framework, refining categories as new data is introduced.

name: The Theoretical Synthesis Engine
command: Present two conflicting theoretical frameworks from the text. In each iteration, identify a shared assumption and a point of irreconcilable difference, then propose a hybrid model that accounts for both sets of data. Repeat the refinement until the hybrid model can successfully explain a recent case study that neither original theory could alone.
what_it_does: Merges two conflicting theories into a unified framework that explains the discrepancies.

name: The Visual-to-Text Translator
command: Describe this complex figure in one paragraph. Then, based solely on your description, predict the key trend and 'lowest point' in the data. If the prediction is wrong, rewrite the description to be more precise. Continue until a reader who cannot see the image could draw a roughly accurate version of the chart based only on your words.
what_it_does: Iteratively describes complex data visualizations until the text alone perfectly conveys the trends.

name: The 1:1 Agenda Optimizer
command: Take my current 1:1 agenda and remove all tactical 'status' items. Replace them with one 'Career Growth' question, one 'Blocker Removal' request, and one 'Feedback Loop' prompt. Keep refining the wording until the agenda signals that I am thinking two levels above my current role. Stop when it's balanced and concise.
what_it_does: Drafts 1:1 agendas that force meaningful career conversations instead of status updates.

name: The BATNA Pressure Loop
command: Act as my manager during a salary negotiation; challenge my request for a raise by offering non-monetary 'perks' or citing budget freezes. For every objection you give, I will provide a counter-rebuttal which you must then critique for assertiveness and logical strength. Continue this roleplay until I have successfully defended my target number against five distinct push-back tactics.
what_it_does: Simulates a defensive manager during salary negotiations to harden your counter-arguments.

name: The Burnout Early-Warning Loop
command: Analyze my sent messages from the past week and flag any instances of passive-aggression, excessive brevity, or pessimism. For each flag, suggest a way to rephrase the sentiment to keep it professional while I address the underlying stress. Continue until you’ve mapped my top three 'stress triggers' and a plan to mitigate each.
what_it_does: Audits your sentiment in Slack/emails to detect signs of professional exhaustion.

name: The Calendar Conflict Resolution Loop
command: Analyze my calendar conflicts and draft a refusal message for the lower-priority meeting that suggests an asynchronous alternative. Iteratively refine the message to ensure it offers a 'partial win' (like a shared doc) while holding the boundary on the time block. Continue until the tone is helpful but immovable.
what_it_does: Drafts firm but polite automated responses to protect deep-work blocks.

name: The Critical Feedback Simulator
command: Adopt the persona of a demanding manager who is unhappy with my project velocity. Attack my recent output with three specific criticisms, then let me respond. After each response, grade my professionalism and suggest how to pivot to a 'solution-oriented' stance. Stop when my responses consistently neutralize your 'threat' messages.
what_it_does: Prepares you for a tough performance review by simulating a hyper-critical manager.

name: The Cross-Functional Translator
command: Analyze my technical project description and identify five terms that would confuse a non-technical stakeholder. Rewrite the entire description focusing on 'User Benefit' and 'Revenue Impact' instead of 'Features.' Iterate until a non-technical person would fully understand the value within one paragraph.
what_it_does: Translates technical jargon into the 'language of business' for non-tech stakeholders.

name: The Delegation Blueprint Loop
command: Analyze my verbal description of a task and break it into a step-by-step Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). For every step, identify a potential error a beginner might make and add a 'sanity check.' Keep refining the steps until the process can be followed with zero clarification questions from a newcomer.
what_it_does: Turns a messy task into a foolproof SOP for a junior or virtual assistant.

name: The Email De-Escalator
command: Analyze this aggressive email thread and suggest three ways to acknowledge the sender's frustration without admitting fault. Rewrite the 'ask' to focus back on the project goal rather than personal blame. Keep iterating until the tone shifts from 'defensive' to 'constructively assertive' and wait for my final approval.
what_it_does: Reduces the 'heat' in a conflict-driven email thread while asserting your point.

name: The Executive Summary Burn
command: Start with my executive summary and cut the word count by 20% in each iteration while retaining all key data points. After each cut, assess if the core 'So What?' is still obvious. Stop only when you reach a 50-word maximum that provides complete situational awareness.
what_it_does: Aggressively condenses long reports into a 'read-in-three-seconds' update for busy VPs.

name: The Executive Voice Mirror
command: Analyze this sample writing from my CEO (or VP) and identify three key linguistic traits (e.g., use of metaphors, data-driven, directness). Rewrite my project proposal using these traits. Iterate until the proposal sounds like something they would have written themselves while maintaining my original content.
what_it_does: Matches your writing style to a specific leader to ensure your proposals get buy-in.

name: The Impact Audit Loop
command: Take a list of my weekly tasks and for each item, draft three increasingly specific metrics (outcome, financial impact, or time saved). Keep refining the wording until every task is expressed as a 'Result-First' bullet point that even a CFO would find undeniable. Continue until 10 bullets meet a 'high-stakes' quality threshold.
what_it_does: Transforms vague tasks into quantifiable business results for performance reviews.

name: The Influence Map Loop
command: List the stakeholders for my project and assign each a 'Power' and 'Interest' score. Iterate through the list to identify the 'Hidden Influencer'—the person who isn't a boss but whose opinion everyone follows. Generate a unique strategy to gain the trust of that specific person by solving one of their pains. Stop when the strategy is actionable.
what_it_does: Identifies the unofficial power players you need to win over for your project.

name: The Internal Referral Loop
command: Take a candidate’s LinkedIn profile and the internal job description, then draft a referral blurb. Focus on specific cultural 'add' rather than 'fit,' and emphasize one unique cross-functional benefit they bring. Iterate until the blurb is compelling enough that a recruiter would prioritize them in under 10 seconds of reading.
what_it_does: Drafts the 'blurb' for internal recruiters to use when you recommend someone.

name: The Interview STAR Loop
command: I will provide an interview story, and you will critique it against the STAR framework. After each critique, I will add more detail or remove fluff until the 'Result' section is the most memorable part of the story. Repeat until the story is under 90 seconds when read aloud and has high emotional resonance.
what_it_does: Refines interview stories to fit the Situation-Task-Action-Result framework perfectly.

name: The Low-Value Task Slayer
command: Look at this recurring task and estimate its annual cost in salary hours. Generate three logical reasons why this task can be automated, outsourced, or eliminated. Continue refining the 'business case for deletion' until it sounds like a cost-saving win for the company rather than a request for less work.
what_it_does: Builds a business case for stopping a task that no longer adds value.

name: The Meeting ROI Filter
command: Look at this meeting agenda and calculate a hypothetical hourly cost based on the number of attendees. Propose three asynchronous ways the same outcome could be achieved for 10% of that cost. Iterate the proposals until you find a format that provides the exact same 'decision-ready' state without a live synchronicity requirement.
what_it_does: Pre-evaluates meeting invites to determine if a meeting is actually necessary.

name: The Network Nurture Loop
command: Identify a person in my network and suggest a specific piece of industry news or a tool that would genuinely help them. Iterate the outreach message until it is under three sentences and requires exactly zero effort for them to respond to. Continue until you’ve generated five personalized 'pings' for five different types of contacts.
what_it_does: Drafts low-friction, high-value 'check-in' messages for old professional contacts.

name: The Peer Feedback Sanitizer
command: Read these peer feedback submissions and categorize the comments into 'Vents,' 'Observations,' and 'Actions.' For each 'Vent,' identify the underlying professional need and rewrite it as a constructive goal. Iterate until you have a clean list of 5 growth priorities free of emotional noise.
what_it_does: Extracts actionable growth points from vague or overly emotional peer reviews.

name: The Political Landmine Loop
command: Analyze this sensitive email draft and identify potential 'ego-threats' or areas where I might be overstepping departmental silos. After each scan, suggest a softer alternative and re-evaluate the risk level until the total friction score is near zero. Repeat this adjustment until the tone achieves a balance of authority and diplomacy.
what_it_does: Identifies hidden status-quo threats and ego-bruising language in multi-departmental emails.

name: The Promotion Checklist Loop
command: Compare my current job description with the one for the next logical level in my career path. List all gaps and generate one 'Level+1' project or responsibility I could take on for each gap. Keep refining the project ideas until they represent high-visibility leadership opportunities rather than just more 'doer' work.
what_it_does: Identifies the exact discrepancy between your current output and the next level's requirements.

name: The Quarterly Goal Refiner
command: I will give you a vague career goal (e.g., 'Get better at Python'). You will iteratively transform it into a 'SMART' goal with three Key Results that are directly tied to company revenue. Keep refining until the goal is so measurable that success is binary—either I hit it or I didn't.
what_it_does: Turns fuzzy personal goals into high-impact OKRs that lead to bonuses.

name: The Resume Re-Inducer
command: Using this target job description and my resume, identify the top three missing keywords. Rewrite my 'Experience' bullets to include these keywords while maintaining 100% truthfulness. Continue iterating until the 'Match Score' between the resume and the job description is over 90% based on semantic relevance.
what_it_does: Rewrites your resume for a specific role without losing your core identity.

name: The Salary Floor Calculator
command: Input my current salary, cost of living, and data from three market sources. Iteratively adjust for inflation, tax brackets, and retirement goals until you produce a 'Minimum Acceptable Offer' for my next move. Then, generate three scripts to pivot the conversation if a recruiter offers lower than this floor.
what_it_does: Calculates your 'Walk-Away' number based on market data and lifestyle costs.

name: The Skill-Staking Loop
command: List my top three technical skills and my top three soft skills, then combine them to create five unique 'hybrid roles' I could pioneer at my company. Each iteration should make the role more specific to the company’s current pain points. Stop when you have a job title and a 3-sentence pitch that sounds irreplaceable.
what_it_does: Combines your existing skills into a unique 'category of one' value proposition.

name: The Upward Management Loop
command: Review my current project list and my manager's stated annual goals, then draft a status report that bridges the two. For every project, keep rewriting the 'Purpose' section until it directly mirrors one of their KPIs. Repeat until every active task is framed as a strategic win for them.
what_it_does: Drafts status updates that explicitly map individual tasks to the manager's personal KPIs.

name: The Weekly Wins Digest
command: Review my daily logs and cluster them into three strategic themes: 'Revenue Impact,' 'Operational Efficiency,' and 'Future Growth.' For each theme, write a one-sentence summary that highlights the most impressive stat. Keep refining until the email takes less than 30 seconds to read and sounds like a major success story.
what_it_does: Aggregates small daily tasks into a high-level Friday email for your boss.

name: The Champion Builder Loop
command: Based on my prospect's internal challenges, draft a 1-page business case they can present to their CFO. Review the draft and remove any mention of our brand name, replacing it with the specific internal outcomes the CFO cares about. Keep refining until the document looks like it was written by the prospect, not by a salesperson.
what_it_does: Creates internal selling materials for your prospect to use with their boss.

name: The Economic Driver Loop
command: Take these three customer metrics and calculate a 12-month ROI. After the first calculation, play devil's advocate and find two reasons why these numbers are unrealistic. Adjust the math and assumptions iteratively until the ROI is conservative enough to be defensible in a procurement audit.
what_it_does: Calculates and defends the financial impact of a purchase.

name: The Friction Point Scraper
command: Review each slide of this sales deck from the perspective of a distracted prospect. For each slide, identify one reason they would stop listening or get confused, then propose a simplification. Keep iterating through the slides until the entire deck can be understood by someone glancing at it for only 5 seconds per slide.
what_it_does: Identifies hidden UX or process friction in your current sales deck.

name: The Gap-Selling Auditor
command: Read these discovery call notes and identify the 'Current State' and 'Future State'. If the gap between them isn't quantified in dollars or hours, suggest three follow-up questions to uncover that data. Repeat this analysis for every pain point mentioned until a clear case for urgency is established.
what_it_does: Evaluates discovery notes to find the 'pain gap' and business impact.

name: The Gatekeeper Whisperer
command: Simulate a protective Executive Assistant. I will try to get a meeting with the CEO. Reject me five times using different common excuses. After each rejection, I will try a new approach (collaboration, referral, low-threat request). Continue until you, as the EA, feel it is 'safer' to let me through than to block me.
what_it_does: Navigating PAs and EAs to get to the true decision-maker.

name: The Insight Lead Loop
command: Take this industry news article and extract one surprising statistic or trend. Draft an email that leads with this insight rather than our product. Iterate until the connection between the insight and our product's primary value prop is clear but not forced.
what_it_does: Turns industry news into an 'educational' hook for prospects.

name: The Loom Script Looper
command: Draft a 60-second script for a personalized video demo. Check the word count; if it's over 130 words, cut it down. Refine it by ensuring the 'personalized' part is in the first 5 seconds. Repeat until the script is punchy, high-energy, and focuses entirely on one specific 'wow' feature.
what_it_does: Optimizes a 60-second video script for maximum engagement.

name: The Loss Interviewer
command: Pretend to be the prospect who just chose a competitor. I will ask you why we lost. Give me the 'polite' answer first, then keep pushing yourself to give the 'brutal' answer (e.g., 'your demo was boring', 'I didn't trust you'). Continue until you've identified three specific areas for improvement in my sales process.
what_it_does: Simulates a lost-deal interview to find the brutal truth.

name: The Mic-Drop Closer
command: Evaluate my current closing statement against our top three competitors. For each competitor, identify one thing they do better and rewrite our statement to neutralize that advantage without naming them. Continue until the statement feels like the only logical conclusion for a rational buyer.
what_it_does: Iterates on a final 'why us' statement to be unassailable.

name: The Micro-Closing Loop
command: Review this discovery call script. At every 3-minute mark, insert a 'micro-close' question (e.g., 'Does that sound like it would solve the problem we discussed yesterday?'). Refine these questions so they are open-ended but guide the prospect toward agreement. Continue until the script feels like a series of small, natural confirmations.
what_it_does: Practices getting small 'yeses' throughout the sales process.

name: The Multi-Threader Architect
command: Examine the current deal status and identify three missing personas (e.g., IT, Procurement, End-user). Draft a specific value proposition for each, then refine it by checking if it conflicts with the primary Champion's goals. Repeat the refinement until you have a cohesive narrative that aligns all stakeholders without causing internal friction.
what_it_does: Maps out a strategy to engage multiple stakeholders within a single account.

name: The Mutual Action Planner
command: Starting from a target go-live date, work backwards to create a Mutual Action Plan (MAP). For every step, add a 'Reason for this step' that focuses on the prospect's benefit. Refine the timeline until no single step takes longer than 5 days, ensuring constant momentum toward the close.
what_it_does: Iteratively designs a timeline that holds the prospect accountable.

name: The Negative Persona Filter
command: Analyze this prospect's profile and list three reasons why they might be a 'bad fit' or a waste of time. For each reason, draft a 'disqualified-style' discovery question to test if that risk is real. Refine the list until you have identified the single most likely reason this deal will fail.
what_it_does: Actively tries to disqualify a lead to save time on dead ends.

name: The Objection Wall Loop
command: Simulate a highly skeptical VP of Finance. After each rebuttal I provide, find a new, deeper reason to reject the ROI until you have exhausted 10 distinct logical fallacies or I successfully isolate the 'true' objection through specific discovery questions. Keep pushing back with increasing intensity until I reach a verbal agreement to proceed.
what_it_does: Stress-tests a pitch by simulating a cynical buyer who rejects every counter-argument.

name: The Price Defense Loop
command: I will give you a price objection (e.g., 'You're too expensive'). Respond by shifting the focus from 'cost' to 'value of inaction'. Keep iterating the response by removing any 'discounting' language until you can justify the full price using the prospect's own stated pain points.
what_it_does: Prepares you to hold firm on pricing without losing the deal.

name: The Referral Engine Loop
command: Analyze this recent successful customer interaction and identify the 'peak moment of delight'. Draft a referral request specifically for that moment. Rewrite the request three times, each time making it easier for the customer to say 'yes' (e.g., providing a template they can copy). Stop once the friction for the customer is near zero.
what_it_does: Optimizes the timing and phrasing of asking for warm introductions.

name: The Rejection Inoculator
command: Give me a list of 10 increasingly rude and dismissive ways a prospect might hang up a cold call. For each one, I will provide a 'graceful exit' or a 'pattern interrupt' to keep the conversation alive. Loop through the list until I can respond to the rudest comment without losing my composure or professional tone.
what_it_does: Builds mental resilience by simulating a rapid-fire series of 'no's.

name: The Signal-to-Noise Prospector
command: Analyze this raw company data and draft a bespoke 2-sentence opening hook. Review your own draft, looking for 'salesy' buzzwords; if any exist, rewrite the hook to be more observational and less transactional. Repeat this refinement process until the hook sounds like a note from a peer rather than a vendor.
what_it_does: Filters raw intent data into highly personalized, non-generic outreach hooks.

name: The Social Proof Weaver
command: Take this customer success story and condense it into a single, punchy sentence. Find a way to weave that sentence into a cold email that addresses a specific pain point. Iterate until the social proof feels like a natural part of the conversation rather than a tacked-on boast.
what_it_does: Integrates case studies into outreach so they don't feel forced.

name: The Territory Mapper
command: Look at this list of 20 accounts. Cluster them by industry and then rank them by 'likelihood to buy now' based on current trends. For the top 3, identify the 'missing piece' of info I need to start a campaign. Iterate the ranking until the highest-effort accounts are justified by the highest potential revenue.
what_it_does: Prioritizes an account list based on 'path of least resistance'.

name: The Trigger Event Watcher
command: Analyze these company updates (new hire, funding, acquisition). For each, brainstorm three different ways this event changes their business priorities. Draft one outreach message for the most likely priority change. Iterate the message until it avoids 'congratulations' and focuses instead on 'impending challenges' caused by the change.
what_it_does: Converts company milestones into immediate outreach opportunities.

name: The Value-Add Sequence
command: Develop a 4-part email nurture sequence where I never ask for a meeting. Each email must provide a specific resource (template, data, intro, insight). Review each email: if there is a 'call to action' to book a meeting, remove it. Continue until the sequence purely establishes me as a 'valuable advisor' in the prospect's inbox.
what_it_does: Creates a series of 'no-ask' emails that build massive reciprocity.

name: Red Team Loop
command: Act as a red team attacking the plan above. List ten ways an adversary, a bad actor, or simple bad luck could break it. For each, rate likelihood (low/med/high) and impact (low/med/high). End with the three failure modes I should mitigate before shipping.
what_it_does: Surfaces failure modes by adversarial review before they hit production.

name: The 60-Second Hook Guillotine
command: Start with my current introduction and cut 20% of the word count in each turn while increasing the 'curiosity gap' and perceived stakes. Continue this pruning and sharpening until the entire intro is under 150 words and starts with a jarring, non-obvious statement. Stop when the first sentence makes it impossible for a listener to hit 'skip'.
what_it_does: Iteratively condenses a long intro into a punchy, high-stakes 60-second opening that demands immediate attention.

name: The Anecdote Salt-Miner
command: Scan the provided transcript and extract one raw story, then iteratively rewrite it into a 'hook-body-lesson' framework for LinkedIn. After each version, remove more corporate jargon and add more sensory details until the story feels visceral. Stop when you have identified and polished three distinct, standalone micro-narratives.
what_it_does: Digs through interview transcripts to find and polish hidden micro-stories that can be repurposed for social media.

name: The Bio-Dynamic Intro
command: Take this guest's bio and iteratively rewrite it as a podcast host's spoken introduction, adding 'credibility anchors' and building excitement. Each iteration must remove one cliché (like 'thought leader') and replace it with a specific, impressive achievement. Stop when the intro sounds like an announcement for a heavyweight champion.
what_it_does: Transforms a standard LinkedIn bio into a cinematic, high-energy guest introduction.

name: The Call-to-Action Escalator
command: Take the current closing statement and iteratively increase the urgency and clarity of the Call to Action. After each draft, remove 'friction' words (like 'maybe' or 'if you want') and replace them with high-certainty verbs. Stop when the closing is under 30 seconds and provides a single, irresistible next step.
what_it_does: Refines the end of a podcast or talk from a weak 'thanks for listening' into a high-conversion directive.

name: The Cold-Open Architect
command: Search the transcript for the moment of highest emotional tension or the most shocking reveal, then iteratively rewrite it into a 'cold open' teaser. Ensure the teaser ends on a literal or figurative cliffhanger before the music would kick in. Stop when the teaser is 45 seconds long and creates an immediate 'need to know'.
what_it_does: Locates the most dramatic moment in an interview and crafts it into a teaser that starts the show.

name: The Curiosity Gap Show Notes
command: Review the episode transcript and generate five 'blind' bullet points that describe a benefit or story without revealing the answer. Iteratively refine these bullets to be shorter and more provocative after each pass, comparing them against the 'curiosity bar' of a top-tier newsletter. Stop when each bullet point creates an itch that can only be scratched by listening.
what_it_does: Transforms boring timestamps into localized cliffhangers that force the reader to listen to the episode.

name: The Echo-Location Edit
command: Analyze this transcript for repetitive sentence starters, 'filler' logic, and redundant transitions like 'basically' or 'to be honest'. Iteratively rewrite the text to vary sentence length and structure after each pass. Stop when no two consecutive sentences start with the same word and all linguistic crutches are eliminated.
what_it_does: Identifies and rewrites repetitive sentence patterns and 'crutch phrases' that make a speaker sound amateur.

name: The High-Stakes Signposter
command: Examine the speech and identify points where a listener might lose focus, then iteratively insert internal summaries and 'bridge' sentences after each section. Refine these signs to be more conversational and less formal with each pass. Stop once there is a clear 'What we just covered / Where we are going' transition every five minutes.
what_it_does: Inserts 'signposts' into long-form talks to ensure listeners never lose the thread of the argument.

name: The Host-Guest Friction Engine
command: Take my main thesis and play the role of a skeptical, highly informed podcast guest who disagrees. Generate a rebuttal, then refine my original point to address that specific weakness, repeating this cycle until the argument is bulletproof. End the loop once you have five 'unanswerable' questions and their corresponding defensive pivots.
what_it_does: Generates increasingly challenging counter-arguments to your own points to prepare for high-level podcast debates.

name: The Intonational Scorecard
command: Evaluate the current script segment and insert performance brackets [e.g., speed up, long pause, whisper] to maximize audience retention. Rewrite the phrasing to sound more like natural speech after each iteration, continuing until the transcript reads as a high-stakes dramatic performance. Stop when every 15-second block has a distinct tonal shift.
what_it_does: Annotates speech scripts with specific performance cues for cadence, pauses, and emotional emphasis until it flows naturally.

name: The Jargon Exorcism
command: Identify every technical term or abstract concept in this keynote section and replace it with a relatable physical metaphor. After each draft, simplify the sentence structure further until the reading grade level is below 7th grade without losing the core truth. Stop when a child could explain the concept back to you based only on your metaphors.
what_it_does: Iteratively simplifies complex technical speech segments into metaphors that any listener can instantly grasp.

name: The Rabbit Hole Interrogator
command: Based on this guest's provided bio or previous answers, generate three standard questions, then iteratively transform them into 'Level-3' questions that ask about the 'why' and 'how' behind their successes. After each iteration, make the questions more specific to their unique history and less likely to have been asked before. Stop when the questions feel like they would surprise the guest themselves.
what_it_does: Analyzes guest answers to generate 'Level 2' and 'Level 3' follow-up questions that avoid surface-level fluff.

name: The Sensory Script Overlay
command: Go through the narrative sections of this script and iteratively replace abstract nouns with sensory descriptions (smell, sound, texture). After each pass, ensure the 'pacing' allows the listener's mind to build the image before moving to the next point. Stop when every major story beat contains at least two non-visual sensory details.
what_it_does: Injects visual, auditory, and tactile descriptions into a script to make stories more immersive for audio-only listeners.

name: The Soundbite Sculptor
command: Select the most important insight from this text and iteratively compress it into a single, rhythmic sentence under 15 words. Use techniques like alliteration, parallel structure, or 'the rule of three' to make it memorable. Stop when the sentence feels like a standalone 'quote card' that requires no further context to be powerful.
what_it_does: Synthesizes long paragraphs into high-impact, rhythmic aphorisms designed to be shared on social media.
