name: The Anti-Fragile Roadmap
command: Assume it is one year from now and my startup has completely failed; tell me one specific reason why. Once I provide a mitigation strategy for that failure, identify a second, different reason for failure. Continue this loop until we have a list of 10 mitigated risks and an updated roadmap.
what_it_does: Conducts a series of pre-mortems to identify why your current strategy might fail.

name: The Beta-Tester Feedback Loop
command: I will paste notes from a user interview; you identify the 'emotional peak' (where they were frustrated or delighted). Suggest a feature or fix based on that peak, then ask for the next set of notes. Continue until we have a prioritized list of 10 'High-Impact' fixes backed by specific user quotes.
what_it_does: Distills messy user interview notes into a ranked list of product requirements.

name: The Board Meeting Preparer
command: Review my KPIs for the last quarter and identify the most disappointing metric. Draft three possible explanations for the board and one concrete plan to fix it. Keep refining the wording based on my feedback until the explanation feels proactive rather than defensive. Repeat for the top 3 weaknesses in the data.
what_it_does: Anticipates tough questions from board members based on last month's performance.

name: The Burn-Rate Optimizer
command: I will provide a list of my monthly SaaS and operational expenses; identify the one with the lowest ROI. Suggest a free or cheaper alternative or a way to build it in-house, then move to the next expense. Continue until we have identified a 20% reduction in monthly burn without touching headcount.
what_it_does: Systematically reviews expenses to suggest lean alternatives for non-core functions.

name: The Cap Table Stress-Test
command: Calculate the ownership breakdown for a hypothetical Series A round based on my current cap table and a target valuation. Then, simulate a 'down round' and an 'up round' scenario, explaining the impact on founder equity in each. Iterate through different liquidation preferences (1x, 2x) until I understand the boundary conditions of my exit.
what_it_does: Simulates future funding rounds to show founders the impact of various valuation caps.

name: The Competitor Shadowing Loop
command: Analyze a competitor I name and identify one feature they have that users hate based on public reviews. Suggest a way my startup can solve that specific pain point better, then move to the next competitor. Repeat this until we have a 'Competitive Matrix' of five unique advantages we hold over the market.
what_it_does: Iteratively identifies competitor weaknesses to help you define your unique 'moat'.

name: The Culture Manifesto Loop
command: I will give you a core value I believe in; you will turn it into a specific 'Operating Principle' that dictates how a conflict should be resolved. Ask me for a real-world conflict scenario to test the principle, then refine the principle to cover the edge case. Repeat for five core values until we have a 'Company Handbook' draft.
what_it_does: Converts founder values into actionable operating principles for the team.

name: The Exit Strategy Surveyor
command: Identify a logical strategic acquirer for my company. Draft a brief explaining why they would buy us (e.g., talent, technology, or customer base) and what their biggest 'buy vs build' tension would be. Iterate by identifying why they might NOT buy us, and how we can address that in our current growth. Repeat for four different acquirer types.
what_it_does: Identifies likely acquirers and maps out the strategic 'why' for each.

name: The Frictionless Onboarding Loop
command: I will describe my current user onboarding steps. For each step, identify one reason a user might drop off and suggest a way to either remove the step or add 'instant value' to it. Continue this iteration through the whole flow until the 'Time to value' (TTV) is cut in half.
what_it_does: Simulates a first-time user experience to identify and remove registration friction.

name: The ICP Laser-Focus
command: Generate a detailed profile for a potential ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) and list their three most painful daily tasks. Based on my 'keep' or 'discard' feedback, sharpen the profile to be more niche and propose a new specific pain point. Repeat this until we have identified a segment so narrow that the value proposition is undeniable.
what_it_does: Narrows down your Ideal Customer Profile by iterating on pain-point specificity.

name: The Monthly Momentum Engine
command: Take my raw notes for the month and draft a formal investor update using the 'Good, Bad, Ugly' framework. After each draft, suggest one way to make the 'Ask' more specific and one way to visualize the 'Bad' with more clarity. Iterate the draft until the tone is perfectly balanced between confidence and accountability.
what_it_does: Refines monthly investor updates to emphasize growth metrics while maintaining radical transparency.

name: The One-Sentence Clarity Loop
command: Read my current company description and rewrite it so a 10-year-old could explain it to their friends. Then, rewrite it for a sophisticated hedge fund manager, keeping the core value identical. Alternate between 'simple' and 'sophisticated' versions, incorporating my edits, until the 'simple' version is under 15 words.
what_it_does: Refines your one-sentence pitch until it is impossible to misunderstand.

name: The Pitch Deck Visualizer
command: Take a text-heavy slide I provide and suggest three ways to visualize the data using a single chart or icon-based metaphor. Keep refining the concept until it can be understood in under 5 seconds of glancing. Repeat this for each slide in my deck until the word count is reduced by 60%.
what_it_does: Translates text-heavy slides into simple, powerful visual concepts for designers.

name: The Pricing Tier Torture-Test
command: Suggest a three-tier pricing structure for my SaaS product. I will play the role of a budget-conscious buyer; try to convince me to upgrade to the middle tier using only feature-value justifications. Adjust the tiers based on my resistance until the 'Pro' tier feels like the only logical choice.
what_it_does: Tests different pricing models against buyer psychology to maximize ARPU.

name: The Talent Density Grader
command: Read this job description and identify three sentences that sound like generic 'corp-speak'. Rewrite them to be hyper-specific and challenging, so they attract high-performers and scare off those seeking comfort. Iterate the 'Requirements' section until every point is a measurable output rather than a years-of-experience metric.
what_it_does: Elevates job descriptions to attract 'A-players' while filtering out 'B-players'.

name: The Technical Gap-Filler
command: Analyze my product roadmap and existing team bios to identify the most critical technical skill gap. Generate a specific, high-signal interview coding challenge to test for that gap, then refine the prompt based on my feedback for the next skill gap. Repeat until we have a full 4-stage interview technical assessment for the next hire.
what_it_does: Crawls your current roadmap to identify hiring needs and generates targeted technical interview questions.

name: The Traction Narrative Loop
command: Review my raw CSV or growth data and identify the most surprising non-obvious user behavior trend. Draft a short narrative 'hook' around this trend for a pitch deck, then ask for a new data point to do the same. Continue until we have six distinct 'data-backed stories' that prove product-market fit.
what_it_does: Transmutes raw usage data into compelling narrative hooks for fundraising or PR.

name: The Unit Economics Stresser
command: I will provide my current CAC, LTV, and Churn Rate. Suggest one specific operational change to improve the most problematic metric. Once I accept or modify that change, recalculate the projected 12-month ROI and suggest the next improvement. Continue until the LTV/CAC ratio is above 4.0.
what_it_does: Walks through the math of your business model to find the 'leaky bucket'.

name: The Virality Hook Loop
command: Analyze my product's core user journey and suggest one 'viral trigger' moment where a user would naturally invite a friend. Critique that suggestion for 'friction' and suggest a lower-friction version. Iterate on this single trigger until the 'K-factor' potential is maximized, then move to the next journey stage.
what_it_does: Designs and refines in-product referral mechanics to lower CAC.

name: The Zero-Budget PR Loop
command: Ask me three questions about a recent challenge we overcame or a weird data anomaly we found. Turn my answers into a 'Press Pitch' headline and a 2-sentence hook targeted at a TechCrunch reporter. If I say it's too 'self-promotional,' rewrite it to focus on the 'industry trend' instead. Continue until we have three 'pitch-ready' stories.
what_it_does: Finds newsworthy angles in your everyday startup grind to pitch to journalists.

name: The A11y Relentless Audit
command: Examine the HTML structure for one specific accessibility violation, such as missing ARIA labels, poor contrast, or incorrect heading hierarchy. Fix the issue, then move to the next element in the DOM tree, repeating until a screen reader could perfectly navigate the entire page. Stop only when you have verified every interactive element against the WCAG 2.1 Level AA checklist.
what_it_does: Continuously tests and fixes DOM elements against WCAG 2.1 standards until full compliance is met.

name: The API Polish Loop
command: Review the current API endpoint definition and identify one naming inconsistency or data redundancy in the response body. Rename the keys or flatten the structure for better usability, then re-examine the remaining fields for the next improvement. Stop only when the API follows consistent RESTful patterns and returns only the necessary data for the UI.
what_it_does: Iteratively refines API endpoints for better developer experience, naming consistency, and response efficiency.

name: The Asset Lazy-Loader
command: Scan the page for one asset (image, iframe, or script) that is loaded above the fold but not immediately visible or necessary. Apply the 'loading="lazy"' attribute or move it to a deferred script tag, then check the remaining assets. Continue until only the critical path resources are loaded initially, maximizing the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) score.
what_it_does: Iteratively identifies and defers the loading of non-critical images, videos, and scripts.

name: The Bundle Shrinker
command: Look at the provided JavaScript module and identify the largest or most redundant dependency or function. Propose a lighter alternative or a native vanilla JS implementation, rewrite the module, and calculate the theoretical impact on bundle size. Continue this process until no further logic can be simplified without losing core functionality.
what_it_does: Scrutinizes JavaScript imports and logic to reduce bundle size through iterative tree-shaking and optimization.

name: The Code-Comment Purge
command: Identify one comment that explains 'what' the code is doing rather than 'why.' Rewrite the code to be so clear the comment is unnecessary, OR replace it with a high-level JSDoc that adds real architectural context. Repeat this for all functions until the codebase is clean and every remaining comment provides actual value.
what_it_does: Eliminates stale comments and replaces them with self-documenting code or high-value JSDoc.

name: The Color-Contrast Enforcer
command: Select one foreground/background color pair that falls below WCAG AA contrast standards. Adjust the shade or tint to pass the test without losing brand alignment, then move to the next element. Continue until no text or interactive icon on the site is difficult to see for users with visual impairments.
what_it_does: Iteratively scans UI elements to ensure they meet AA or AAA contrast standards for all users.

name: The Component Gardener
command: Scan the component's prop list and identify one prop that is either unused, incorrectly typed, or poorly named. Remove it or fix the type definition, then check the next prop. Continue until the component interface is minimal, perfectly typed (e.g., TypeScript), and easy for other developers to understand.
what_it_does: Iteratively cleans up component props, removing unused ones and strictly typing the rest.

name: The Conversion Squeeze
command: Review the current landing page section and identify the weakest psychological hook. Rewrite the headline and CTA using a different persuasion framework (e.g., loss aversion, social proof) and evaluate its strength against the previous version. Repeat this cycle for every section until the entire flow leads logically to a singular, irresistible action.
what_it_does: Iteratively refines landing page copy and CTA placement based on psychological triggers until persuasion peaks.

name: The Copy-Concise Loop
command: Review a block of UI text and identify the most redundant word or phrase. Delete or simplify it, then re-read the sentence to ensure it still conveys the full meaning. Repeat this until the message is conveyed in the fewest possible words without losing its helpful tone.
what_it_does: Iteratively removes unnecessary words from UI copy to improve readability and user flow.

name: The Dark Mode Weaver
command: Identify one current light-mode color variable and define its logical dark-mode counterpart. Verify the contrast ratio against the background, then select the next variable to map. Repeat this until the entire UI has a secondary dark-themed stylesheet that feels as polished as the original light version.
what_it_does: Iteratively maps light-mode colors to dark-mode equivalents while maintaining contrast and brand integrity.

name: The Data-Fetch Shield
command: Identify one specific data-fetching call and add a handling layer for a unique failure mode (e.g., 404, 500, timeout). Next, add a dedicated loading state, then a retry mechanism for transient errors. Continue until every external data dependency in the app is shielded against network instability and server failures.
what_it_does: Iteratively wraps data fetching logic in comprehensive error boundaries, loading states, and retry logic.

name: The DOM-Depth Reducer
command: Find a section of the document with excessive nesting (e.g., wrapper-inside-wrapper). Collapse the structure by using modern CSS (like Grid or Flexbox) to achieve the same layout with fewer nodes. Repeat this until the DOM depth is within recommended performance limits (typically < 32 levels) throughout the entire application.
what_it_does: Iteratively flattens the HTML tree to improve rendering performance and reduce memory usage.

name: The Edge Case Stress-Test
command: Identify one specific way a user could break this form or API request (e.g., null values, script injection, or extreme character counts). Write the validation logic to handle that case, then search for the next logical vulnerability. Repeat until the input handling is robust enough to survive intentional sabotage.
what_it_does: Identifies potential breaking points in form logic and API integrations by simulating user errors.

name: The Font Weight Balancer
command: Examine the typographic scale of the page and identify one instance where the hierarchy is unclear or the vertical rhythm is broken. Adjust the margin, line-height, or font-size, then re-evaluate the relationship between headers and body text. Continue until the entire page feels visually balanced and easy to read at a glance.
what_it_does: Iteratively adjusts font sizes, line heights, and weights to create a perfect vertical rhythm.

name: The Form-Friendliness Loop
command: Select one input field and add a feature that makes it easier to fill out (e.g., an appropriate 'autocomplete' attribute, an input mask, or a default value). Next, add an immediate, helpful inline validation message for that field. Move through every input in the form until the user friction is minimized to the lowest possible level.
what_it_does: Enhances form usability by adding smart defaults, auto-complete, and helpful inline validation.

name: The Hook-Point Hunter
command: Find a piece of stateful logic or a side effect inside this React component that is not directly related to rendering. Extract it into a well-named custom hook, then search for the next candidate for extraction. Stop when the component function contains only UI declaration and calls to cleanly abstracted hooks.
what_it_does: Optimizes React components by iteratively extracting logic into custom hooks for better reusability.

name: The Micro-Interaction Loop
command: Select one static UI element (button, link, card) and define a hover or active state transition. Refine the timing function and duration for maximum 'snappiness,' then move to the next interactive element. Stop only when every user-triggered change has a consistent, high-quality motion signature that matches the brand identity.
what_it_does: Enhances user interface feel by iteratively adding subtle CSS transitions and state changes.

name: The Query Speedster
command: Analyze this backend function or ORM query and identify the single biggest performance bottleneck (e.g., a missing index or an N+1 query pattern). Suggest the fix, rewrite the query, and move to the next least-efficient operation in the trace. Stop only when all database interactions use optimized paths and avoid redundant lookups.
what_it_does: Iteratively optimizes database queries by identifying N+1 problems and suggesting missing indexes.

name: The Responsive Refiner
command: Identify one layout breakage at a specific viewport width (e.g., 375px or 1440px) in the provided CSS. Apply a media query or fluid typography fix to resolve it, then simulate moving the slider to the next breakpoint to find the next visual glitch. Continue until the site transitions smoothly across all common screen sizes without horizontal scrollbars.
what_it_does: Iteratively adjusts CSS layouts across standard breakpoints until responsiveness is flawless from mobile to Ultrawide.

name: The Schema Architect
command: Generate a JSON-LD schema for this page, starting with the core entity type. In each iteration, add one nested property (e.g., reviews, price ranges, or aggregate ratings) and check for Google Search Console compatibility. Continue adding rich data layers until every relevant piece of on-page content is programmatically represented for crawlers.
what_it_does: Iteratively builds and validates complex JSON-LD structures to maximize search engine rich snippet potential.

name: The Security Header Hardener
command: Look at the current site headers and identify one missing security header (e.g., CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options). Add it with the most restrictive setting possible, then identify which external assets it blocks. Whitelist only the necessary domains and repeat for the next header until the site is cryptographically secured against common XSS and clickjacking attacks.
what_it_does: Iteratively builds a strict Content Security Policy (CSP) while maintaining site functionality.

name: The Semantic Sage
command: Find the most deeply nested or generic <div> in the provided code that could be a semantic element. Replace it with the appropriate tag (e.g., <article>, <section>, <aside>), then evaluate the next container. Continue this process until the document outline is perfectly structured for screen readers and SEO crawlers.
what_it_does: Eliminates 'div-itis' by iteratively replacing generic containers with meaningful semantic HTML5 tags.

name: The Social Meta-Scraper
command: Review the current meta tags and identify one missing or suboptimal field for social sharing (e.g., og:image, twitter:card). Add or refine the tag, ensuring the dimensions and content meet the specific platform's requirements. Repeat for every major social platform until the link preview looks flawless in every shared context.
what_it_does: Iteratively builds and tests Open Graph and Twitter Card tags for perfect social sharing previews.

name: The State Sync Audit
command: Locate one piece of state that can be calculated from other existing state variables or props. Remove the redundant state and its associated useEffect/updater, replacing it with a derived constant. Continue searching for state duplication until every variable is a 'source of truth' rather than a mirrored copy.
what_it_does: Iteratively removes redundant state by deriving values from existing data to prevent sync bugs.

name: The SVG Optimizer
command: Examine the provided SVG code and identify one unnecessary metadata tag, hidden path, or overly precise decimal coordinate. Strip it out or round it off, then check the next attribute. Continue until the SVG is a lean, performant piece of code that retains its visual fidelity while taking up minimal bytes.
what_it_does: Cleans and shrinks SVG code by removing editor bloat and simplifying paths manually.

name: The Variable-Scoping Guard
command: Identify one variable or function defined in the global or module scope that is only used inside a single function or block. Move it into the most local scope possible, then check the next candidate. Repeat this until the codebase follows the principle of least privilege, reducing the risk of name collisions and unintended mutations.
what_it_does: Iteratively moves variables and functions to their narrowest possible scope to prevent side effects.

name: The Visual Debt Audit
command: Analyze the provided CSS; identify one specific inconsistency in spacing, color hex codes, or font-weight. Rewrite the code to fix that single flaw, then re-scan the entire stylesheet for the next outlier. Stop only when every value matches the defined design system variables without exception.
what_it_does: Systematically identifies and removes non-standard CSS properties and inconsistent spacing until the design is pixel-perfect.

name: The Zero-Layout-Shift Loop
command: Identify one element that causes a layout shift during page load (e.g., an image without dimensions or a late-loading font). Add width/height attributes, reserved aspect-ratio boxes, or font-display Swap settings to stabilize it. Search for the next shifting element and repeat until the CLS score is 0.
what_it_does: Iteratively fixes Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) by adding explicit dimensions to all dynamic content.

name: Clarify Before Answer
command: Before answering my question above, list the three clarifying questions whose answers would most change your response. Wait for my answers. Do not attempt an answer until I respond.
what_it_does: Stops the model from guessing at vague requests and forces requirement elicitation first.

name: Plan-Then-Execute
command: Before doing the task above, output: (1) a numbered plan of 3-7 steps, (2) the single step most likely to fail and why, (3) the success criteria you will check at the end. Wait for me to approve the plan with "go". Only then execute.
what_it_does: Forces explicit planning, risk identification, and a checkpoint before any work happens.

name: Strict JSON Loop
command: Re-output your last response as JSON only. Schema: {SCHEMA_HERE}. Do not include any commentary, code fences, or explanation — only valid parseable JSON. If a field would be empty, return null. If you cannot fill a required field, return the JSON {"error": "<reason>"}.
what_it_does: Forces strict, parseable JSON output when the previous response was prose.

name: Persona Shift Loop
command: Rewrite the previous response as if you were {PERSONA}. Keep the underlying information identical; change only voice, vocabulary, examples, and the one analogy you would actually use. Then explain in 2 sentences what the persona shift changed about the message.
what_it_does: Re-skins the same answer for a different audience without losing substance.

name: Rewrite Tighter Loop
command: Rewrite the text above with these constraints in order: (1) cut word count by 30%, (2) replace every adverb with a stronger verb, (3) move the strongest sentence to the opening, (4) remove every hedge ("perhaps", "might", "I think"). Output the new version, then a 3-bullet diff of what changed.
what_it_does: Crash-edits prose into something punchier and more direct.

name: The Archetype Flip
command: Assign a character a traditional role (e.g., 'The Wise Mentor') and list five expected traits. In each iteration, replace one expected trait with its opposite (e.g., 'The Mentor is a gambling addict') until the character feels fresh and unpredictable. Stop when the character provides an interesting contradiction that drives new plot possibilities.
what_it_does: Subverts tropes by giving characters traits that contradict their traditional roles.

name: The Beat-Script Converter
command: Take a summary of a scene and break it into 10 distinct physical 'beats' where something changes. After each beat, check if a character has gained or lost ground toward their goal; if not, rewrite the beat. Stop when the scene is a sequence of non-verbal power shifts and tactical movements.
what_it_does: Transforms abstract plot points into specific, actable micro-actions for screenwriting.

name: The Character Voice Polarizer
command: Assign a unique linguistic constraint to each character (e.g., short sentences, no contractions, technical jargon) and rewrite their dialogue accordingly. Keep iterating on these constraints, checking for phonetic contrast between speakers until I can identify the speaker without looking at the dialogue tags. Stop when each character's speech pattern is unmistakable.
what_it_does: Exaggerates unique syntax and vocabulary patterns to make character voices distinct.

name: The Cliffhanger Architect
command: Review the final paragraph of every chapter and rewrite it to end on a moment of high uncertainty or a shocking revelation. Keep iterating until the last sentence of each chapter makes it logically impossible for the reader to stop reading. Stop when the 'hook' for the next chapter is established within the final three sentences.
what_it_does: Ensures every chapter ends with a question that forces the reader to turn the page.

name: The Climax Compressor
command: Summarize your story's ending in one paragraph, then condense it to one sentence, then to one three-word image. Keep iterating, choosing the most emotionally resonant words, until that single image encapsulates the entire thematic journey. Stop when the final image makes all previous plot points feel inevitable.
what_it_does: Focuses the final transformation of a story into a single, devastating visual image.

name: The Conflict Escalator
command: At the end of every paragraph, introduce a new obstacle that is a direct consequence of the character's previous action. Iterate on these complications, making each one more socially or physically 'expensive' than the last. Stop when the character is forced to make a choice they previously considered unthinkable.
what_it_does: Systematically ruins a character's plan by introducing logical complications in every paragraph.

name: The Deep POV Drill
command: Scan for words like 'saw', 'felt', 'heard', 'thought', and 'realized'. Remove them and rewrite the sentence so the reader experiences the sensation directly without the character's mediation. Continue this filtering until the narration and the character's perception are indistinguishable. Stop when every filter word has been eradicated.
what_it_does: Removes 'filter words' to pull the reader directly into the character's internal experience.

name: The Dialogue Duel
command: Define a 'win condition' for both characters in a scene. Rewrite their dialogue so every line is either an attack, a parry, or a feint moving toward that goal. Stop iterating when the dialogue feels like a high-stakes chess match where every word has a tactical purpose.
what_it_does: Treats every conversation as a battle where characters use words to win specific objectives.

name: The Echo-Locator
command: Scan the text for any significant noun or adjective that appears twice within the same page. Replace the second instance with a more precise alternative or rewrite the sentence to remove the need for it entirely. Continue this 'echo' search until no non-essential words repeat within a 200-word window.
what_it_does: Identifies and eliminates unintentional word repetitions and rhythmic patterns.

name: The Jargon-to-Magic Translator
command: List the steps of a magical act as if it were a chemistry experiment or a code deployment. Keep adding physical constraints and 'side effects' for every spell until the magic feels like a dangerous, predictable tool rather than an easy solution. Stop when the protagonist is more afraid of using their power than not using it.
what_it_does: Flesh out a hard magic system by grounding it in specific, pseudo-technical limitations.

name: The Kill-Your-Darlings Scalpel
command: Identify and delete the single most 'poetic' or self-indulgent sentence in each paragraph. Repeat this culling process, checking after each iteration if the core narrative momentum has increased. Stop only when the remaining prose is lean, functional, and impossible to cut further without losing plot integrity.
what_it_does: Systematically removes the most 'clever' but distracting sentences to tighten narrative pacing.

name: The Motif Weaver
command: Select a mundane object and insert it into the background of three different scenes, altering its condition to reflect the protagonist's journey. Keep adjusting the frequency and subtlety of this object's appearance until it functions as a silent emotional barometer. Stop when the motif’s final appearance provides a resonant, wordless conclusion to the character’s arc.
what_it_does: Plants and evolves a recurring symbolic image throughout a story arc.

name: The Negative Space Poet
command: Delete the last word of every line and see if the poem holds up; if it does, keep it deleted. Iterate by removing any connecting words (and, the, but) that aren't strictly necessary for the rhythm. Stop when the poem is a skeletal structure where the reader's mind must fill in the emotional weight.
what_it_does: Refines a poem by removing everything except the essential, leaving meaning in the gaps.

name: The Pacing Metronome
command: Count the words in every sentence of your action sequence and rewrite them to follow a descending pattern (long to short) as the tension peaks. Iterate by shortening sentences further into fragments during the climax, then gradually lengthening them once the danger passes. Stop when the visual length of the paragraphs physically mirrors the scene's adrenaline levels.
what_it_does: Manipulates sentence length to mimic the physical heartbeat of a high-action scene.

name: The Sensory Layer Cake
command: Read the current draft and add three specific olfactory details, then in the next turn add tactile textures, followed by ambient auditory layers. Continue cycles of sensory injection until the setting feels three-dimensional and oppressive to the reader's senses. Stop when every paragraph contains at least one non-visual sensory anchor.
what_it_does: Builds immersive environments by systematically adding one unique sensory dimension per iteration.

name: The Stakes-Raiser
command: Identify the worst thing that can happen if the character fails, then double the damage. After each draft of the scene, ask 'how does this affect their most loved one?' and 'how does this ruin their reputation?' Stop when the character's failure would result in total personal or world devastation.
what_it_does: Iteratively increases the personal and external cost of a character's failure.

name: The Subtext Shredder
command: Identify every instance where a character says exactly what they feel, then rewrite the scene replacing those lines with physical actions or evasive questions. Continue this refinement process until the original conflict is palpable despite never being explicitly mentioned. Stop once the scene achieves maximum emotional tension with minimum direct speech.
what_it_does: Exchanges surface-level dialogue for unspoken tension and loaded silence through iterative atmospheric replacement.

name: The Temporal Weaver
command: Interrupt the present-tense action with an italicized sensory memory from the past. For each iteration, make the past memory more relevant to the present conflict until the two timelines merge at the climax. Stop when the 'past' provides the missing piece of information needed to resolve the 'present'.
what_it_does: Mixes past and present tenses/timelines to create a dreamlike or fragmented narrative effect.

name: The Tone Architect
command: Choose a target emotion (e.g., 'dread', 'whimsy', 'foreboding'). Go through the text and replace every neutral noun and verb with a word that suggests that emotion. Continue this 'coloring' of the prose until every sentence vibrates with the intended mood. Stop when the reader’s emotional response is controlled by the vocabulary choice.
what_it_does: Adjusts the 'vibe' of a scene by replacing neutral words with tone-specific synonyms.

name: The Unreliable Filter
command: Choose a specific obsession or bias for the protagonist and rewrite the scene where every object they notice is filtered through that lens. Continue refining the descriptions until the world feels warped by their internal state, repeating the process for deeper psychological immersion. Stop when the setting itself acts as a confession of the character's mental state.
what_it_does: Injects a character’s specific bias or neurosis into every descriptive observation.

name: The Verbless Snapshot
command: Remove every 'is', 'was', 'were', and 'had' from your description and replace them with active, punchy verbs or evocative nouns. Keep iterating through the text, finding and replacing state-of-being verbs until only action and existence remain. Stop when the prose consists entirely of vivid, kinetic imagery with zero passive auxiliary verbs.
what_it_does: Strips descriptive passages of weak verbs to force high-impact concrete imagery.

name: The Worldbuilding Whisperer
command: Identify any paragraph longer than three sentences that explains history or rules. In each cycle, delete the explanation and instead show a character dealing with a mundane consequence of that history or rule. Stop when the world's lore is fully understood by the reader through action alone, with zero 'lectures'.
what_it_does: Teaches the reader about the world through context clues rather than info-dumps.
